The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be used for the payment of debts owed to the Crown. Where applicable, information pertaining to an individual’s indebtedness may be shared with the individual’s debtors and the courts to facilitate attachment. Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with income tax legislation and/or signed information-sharing agreements, including Canada Border Services Agency (Administrative Monetary Penalty System Program, CBSA PPU 001 and Integrated Customs Enforcement System I.C.E.S., CBSA PPU 016), Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data - Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy, FIN PPU 100) for policy analysis, research and evaluation of the Equalization program, National Research Council Canada (Industrial Research Assistance Program, NRC PPU 026), Statistics Canada (for Business surveys), Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (Inquiries and Compliance Activities, IC PPU 050), Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010 and Family Orders and Agreements Enforcement Assistance, JUS PPU 125), Employment and Social Development Canada (Investigations ESDC  PPU 171), and Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments, PWGSC PCU 712). Information may also be disclosed to provincial governments (or parts thereof) to the extent authorized by law. Personal information obtained about residents of countries with which Canada has Income Tax Conventions may be shared with those taxing authorities by way of an exchange of information under the appropriate treaty provision. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs, including: Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Charities Program (CRA PPU 200), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223), Authentication and Credential Management Service (CRA PPU 607), Non Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220), Scientific Research and Experimental Development (CRA PPU 441), Film and Media Tax Credit Program (CRA PPU 442), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), Advance Income Tax Rulings and Technical Interpretations (CRA PPU 090), Canada Pension Plan and Employment Insurance Rulings Program (CRA PPU 070), Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120), EFILE Online Services (CRA PPU 211), Remissions (CRA PPU 142), Registered Deferred Income and Savings Plans (CRA PPU 226), Monitoring of Electronic Access to Taxpayer Information (CRA PPU 718), GST/HST Rulings and Interpretations (CRA PPU 092), Excise and Specialty Tax (CRA PPU 062), GST/HST Audit and Examination (CRA PPU 430), and GST/HST Returns and Rebates Processing Program (CRA PPU 241). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as: the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (data matching, quality assurance and data integrity), statistics, compliance and enforcement risk management (including risk assessment and business intelligence analytics), strategy development, and reports. The social insurance number may be disclosed to federal, provincial, and/or international partners for the purpose of identification when authorized by legislation. Retention and Disposal Standards : Records are kept for 10 years after the last administrative action, and then destroyed. ### CRA Internal Identity and Credential Management Services
Description: This bank describes personal information related to identification and authentication services for validating the credentials of internal users of the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) computing infrastructure including password management, the issuance of identity based Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) certificates. Certificates are used for encryption of data as well as for identification and authentication of individuals. Some certificates can be used for Digital Signature purposes. Personal information may include: name, contact information, personal record identifier (PRI) or non-Government employee identifier (NGE), unique identification code (User ID), username, partial dates of birth, employment status, tenure type, password and password recovery questions (recovery secrets), and signature. Identity and access management information may be obtained from the employee personnel file as described in Employee Personnel Record PSE 901. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide his/her PRI. Certificates are stored on computers, Smart Cards or other secure media. This PIB was updated in 2016 and was previously entitled Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) External Client Identification (CRA PPU 165) and now contains information described in the former PIB Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) Internal Client Identification (CRA PPU 216). Class of Individuals: CRA employees, consultants, temporary help, students, and individuals hired under contract to work on behalf of CRA, as well as other individuals who are enrolled in CRA applications and/or communicate with CRA employees. Purpose: Personal information is used to authenticate the identity of individuals, to register and assign a unique digital identifier for users, and to authorize, grant, review, revise, or withdraw access privileges to CRA systems and applications. Personal information is collected under the authority of paragraph 30(1)(a) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act, which grants the CRA responsibility for "general administrative policy" at the Agency. Personal information is also collected as required under the Policy on Government Security, in line with the agreement reached with the President of the Treasury Board. Consistent Uses: The information may be used for electronic network monitoring purposes (see Standard PIB Electronic Network Monitoring – PSU 905. In the case of suspected security or privacy breaches, information may be shared with departmental security and access to information and privacy officials (see Standard PIB Security Incidents-PSU 939). For suspected criminal activity, information may also be disclosed to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police for investigation purposes - see institution-specific PIB Operational Case Records - RCMP PPU 005. Information relating to employees from other institutions may be shared with their respective institution. The information may also be used for statistical, research, planning, reporting, audit and evaluation purposes. Information including given name, surname, and e-mail address is published in a public repository in the Public Key Infrastructure x.500 directory and may be referred to in Certificate Revocation Lists. This public repository can be accessed from the Internet by using a Lightweight Directory Application Protocol Browser. Retention and Disposal Standards: Certificate issued for the purpose of encryption and authentication is retained for ten (10) years following the sun setting of the certificate program. Certificates issued for the purpose of authentication only will be retained for a minimum of two (2) years following the expiry or revocation of the certificates. This is done in accordance with the Digital Signature and Confidentiality Certificate Policies for the Government of Canada PKI, V4.0 ( April 3, 2006 ) and the Digital Signature and Confidentiality Certificate Policies for the Canada Revenue Agency PKI, V4.0 ( August 25, 2006 ). All personal information collected for the purpose of issuing or revoking any certificate will be retained for a minimum of two (2) years following the expiry or revocation of the certificates. The retention of password and recovery questions is under development. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Record Number: PRN 932 TBS Registration Number: 004486 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 165

### Criminal Investigations
Description: This bank describes personal information that may be collected in the conduct of criminal investigations into suspected cases of fraudulent non-compliance with the Income Tax Act (ITA), the Excise Tax Act (ETA) or any Act where CRA has been granted authority to conduct investigations. Personal information may be collected indirectly from sources other than the individual under investigation, including witnesses, third parties, informants, media reports and other public sources, credit bureaus, and other federal departments. Personal information may include: name, contact information, financial information, signature, criminal checks/history, fingerprints, social insurance number, business number, and other identification numbers such as driver’s licence number and vehicle registration number, citizenship, credit history, date and place of birth, international travel history, biographical information, opinion or views of individuals, photographs, video/audio recordings and any other document or information relevant to an investigation. It may also include employee personnel information. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide their social insurance number or business number. This personal information bank was updated in 2024 and was formerly entitled Detection and Investigations. Class of Individuals: Individuals suspected of fraudulent non-compliance with the Income Tax Act (ITA), the Excise Tax Act (ETA) or any Act where CRA has been granted authority to conduct investigations, witnesses, third parties, potentially affiliated individuals, their dependents, spouses, common-law partners, representatives, confidential informants and employees. Purpose: Personal information is used to conduct criminal investigations into suspected cases of fraudulent non-compliance with the Income Tax Act (ITA), the Excise Tax Act (ETA) or any Act where CRA has been granted authority to conduct investigations such as the Canada Recovery Benefits Act and the Canada Worker Lockdown Benefit Act. The social insurance number is collected in accordance with the Income Tax Act and the Excise Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. CRA investigators can also obtain evidence by way of search warrants and production orders pursuant to section 487 of the Criminal Code, and may also obtain a court order to compel a taxpayer who has been charged with criminal tax offences to be fingerprinted pursuant to paragraph 2(1)(c) of the Identification of Criminals Act. Information gathering activities may include the execution of search warrants, the use of forensic software to extract data from seized computers or other electronic devices, witness interviews, any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching, knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, and  business intelligence analysis activities. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation, including Canada Border Services Agency (International and Large Business, and Intelligence Program, CBSA PPU 035 ), Canada Employment Insurance Commission, Employment and Social Development Canada (Social Insurance Number Register, ESDC PPU 390 ), Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada (Financial Analysis and Disclosures, FINTRAC PPU 02 0), Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (Regular and official Passports, IRCC PPU 081 ), Public Prosecution Service of Canada (Prosecutions and Prosecution-related Activities PPSC PPU 002 ), Department of Justice Canada (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services JUS PPU 010 ), the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (Identification Services and Canadian Criminal Real Time Identification Services RCMP PPU 030 ) for the purposes of law enforcement (Operational Case Records RCMP PPU 005 ). Via the CRA’s Competent Authority Program (CRA PPU 085 ) or the Department of Justice’s International Assistance Group, the information may also be disclosed with various foreign governments subject to tax treaties, tax information exchange agreements, or Mutual Legal Assistance Treaties. Information may also be disclosed to other applicable provincial/territorial governments (or parts thereof) and international partners in accordance with signed information sharing agreements as authorized by law. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs/Activities, including: Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005 ), Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047 ), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421 ), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035 ), Offshore Tax Informant Program (CRA PPU 411 ), Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085 ), Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) (CRA PPU140 ), Scientific Research and Experimental Development (CRA PPU 441 ), Collections (CRA PPU 050 ), Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Court (CRA PPU 172 ), Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025 ), and Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120 ). Information may be shared with law enforcement agencies, where permitted by law. Information may also be used for program evaluation, strategy development, reporting, and for statistical purposes. Information may also be used to assess the employees’ ability to be a credible witness in Court. Retention and Disposal Standards: Investigation case files retention rule is ten years after a case is closed, completed, approved, after which they are destroyed.
Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA CPB 820 TBS Registration Number: 002752 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 095

### Disability Tax Credit Program
Description: This bank describes personal information related to individuals who apply for the Disability Tax Credit Program. Personal information may include: name (including previous names), signature, contact information, biographical information, social insurance number, date of birth, and medical information. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide: information to confirm authorization, including the social insurance number. This personal information bank was updated in 2024. Class of Individuals: Individuals or authorized representatives who apply for the Disability Tax Credit for themselves, a spouse or a dependent, their parents, guardians, supporting family member, caregivers and medical practitioners. Taxpayers who claim a disability amount on income tax returns for themselves, their spouse, a caregiver, or a dependent. Purpose: Personal Information is used for the following purpose: to administer the Disability Tax Credit program through processing claims, eligibility determination, enforcement, and validation activities. Validation activities may include authorized automated personal information analysis to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA. Personal information may be used for data-matching purposes with information from the Canada Child Benefit and Related Benefits (CRA PPU 063) or the Children’s Special Allowances (CRA PPU 620) to determine if the Child Disability Benefit should be granted as a supplement and with the Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005) to determine eligibility for various tax credits including COVID-19 relief measures governed by legislative authority Personal Information is collected under the authority of section 118, 220, and 241(4)h.1 of the Income Tax Act. The social insurance number  is collected pursuant to subsection 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation and/or signed information sharing agreements, including Employment and Social Development Canada (personal information bank pending) for the administration of a one-time payment to persons with disabilities for reasons related to COVID-19 relief measures, and for the Canada Disability Savings Program (ESDC PPU 038) Also with Statistics Canada for statistical analysis (StatCan PPU 136). Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs, including: Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits (CRA PPU 140), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Canada child benefit and related benefits (CRA PPU 063), Children’s Special Allowances (CRA PPU 620), Individual Returns and payment processing (CRA PPU 005), Detection and Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Income Tax and Charity Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Working Income Tax Benefit (CRA PPU 178) for the coordination and the administration of programs. Information may also be used or shared for other purposes such as: for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance, data integrity, research and statistical analysis), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, and reports. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting. The social insurance number may be disclosed to Employment and Social Development Canada for the purpose of identifying specific individual’s eligibility for the Registered Disability Savings Plan. The social insurance number may be disclosed to Statistics Canada for the purpose of identifying an individual for statistical analysis. Retention and Disposal Standards: Paper records will be retained at a private-sector contractor records storage facility for a period of ten years following the last administrative action and then destroyed. Electronically data transferred files are kept for a period of 2 years then archived for another 2-15 years defending on the file. Retention for the COVID-19 one-time payment for people with disabilities records were kept for a period of two years and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA ABSB 647 TBS Registration Number: 20090478 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 218
Description: This bank describes personal information related to the administration of the Discounters program including regulating the practice of tax discounting to protect the rights and interests of individuals who use discounting services to get their income tax refunds. Personal information may include: name, contact information, language, financial information, other identification numbers such as discounter number and EFILE number, signature, and social insurance number. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the discounter’s identification code. Information may be stored in the following CRA internal systems or databases: the Tax Rebate Discounting Act system, the FileNet system, the Discounters program filing system, and the CRA Electronic Letter Creation System. Class of Individuals: Tax preparers who register to obtain a discounter code, their employees, their clients, and any person having management and control of the applicants such as sole proprietor, branch manager, partner, director, officer and/or controlling shareholder. Purpose: Personal information is collected under the authority of section 5 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act, subsection 4(2) and subparagraph 6(b)(ii) of the Tax Rebate Discounting Act, and is used for the following purposes: processing the registration of discounters including screening of tax discounters, the audit of their discounting transactions, and providing information to discounters. The Social Insurance Number is collected pursuant to the Income Tax Act, the Canada Revenue Agency Act, the Tax Rebate Discounting Act, and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs/Activities, including: Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), EFILE Online Services (CRA PPU 211), Collection Actions (CRA PPU 050), and Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095). Lastly, information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails). ### EFILE Online Services
Description: This bank describes personal information related to the registration, suitability screening and the monitoring of electronic filers, and the management of credentials used to provide authentication services for secure online program applications related to EFILE. EFILE is an automated service that permits those who prepare and file taxes on behalf of others to electronically file tax and benefit returns to the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) directly from the software used to prepare the tax return. Personal information may include: name, contact information, Social Insurance Number (SIN), criminal checks, tax compliance history, financial information, residency, language preference, business number, discounter number, Internet Protocol (IP) Address, email address, EFILE number and password. Personal information will be verified against sources within the CRA. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide their SIN and EFILE number. Information may be stored in the following CRA internal systems or databases: Efiler Information system (EIS), Electronic Filer List Program (ELP) and the Efiler Postal Code Search (EPCS). This Personal Information Bank (PIB) was updated in 2022. Class of Individuals: EFILE applicants (including sole proprietors, partners in partnerships and corporate officers), representatives and designated authorities, discounters and clients of electronic filers. Purpose: Personal information is used to authenticate the identity of applicants and screen them for suitability and to issue, manage, and authenticate the EFILE number and password. The personal information is collected under the authority of sections 150 and 220 of the Income Tax Act (ITA). The SIN is collected pursuant to section 237 of the ITA and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with the province of Quebec (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed information sharing agreements. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with the following internal CRA Programs: Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Community Volunteer Income Tax Program (CRA PPU 100), Discounters Program (CRA PPU 212), and Monitoring of Electronic Access to Taxpayer Information (CRA PPU 718). Lastly, information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, and reporting and for survey or other statistical analysis purposes. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained for 6 years and then destroyed. For problematic cases, where the information may be required in the future, the retention time could be up to 10 years. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA ABSB 216 TBS registration: 20170136 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 211
Description: This bank describes personal information related to the registration, withholding, remitting, and reporting obligations for payroll deductions, GST/HST on employee benefits, and other levies, as well as assessments levied for failure to deduct and remit amounts, including penalties and interest. This would include all accounting transactions with respect to employees’ source deductions, employers’ remittances and reconciliation, and the verification of corporate returns to ensure various claims such as, but not limited to, travel expenses, professional and consulting fees, meals and entertainment are claimed accurately on the corporate returns, in order to address non-compliance where applicable. Personal information may be collected indirectly from sources other than the taxpayer, including open-source Internet checks and other public sources, payroll service bureaus, and other provincial and federal departments. Personal information may include: contact information (including street name, street number, city, province, territory, postal code, e-mail addresses, telephone, fax, cell phone numbers), date of birth, date of death, employee identification number, employee personnel information (including salary, wages, bonuses, director’s fees, hours worked, deductions, percentage of voting shares controlled, type or work/employment work arrangements, working relationship, control, ownership of tools and equipment, financial risks, subcontracting work), financial information (including financial institution information for direct deposit and other banking purposes),, biographical information, citizenship status, credit history, gender, medical information (including Date or periods of disability, whether employees are considered disabled under CPP or QPP), name, other identification numbers (including Business number (BN), Payroll account number, and representative ID), Signature, and Social Insurance Number (SIN). Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide the necessary authorization and adequate proof of identification, including full name, BN, SIN, Representative ID, and date of birth. This PIB was updated in 2025. Class of Individuals : Individuals, sole proprietors, partners in partnerships and their employees, authorized corporate officers and their employees, and authorized representatives. Purpose: Personal information is used for the following purpose: review or examine the books and records of businesses, including payroll accounts, to ensure that businesses are compliant with filing, remitting, reporting and withholding requirements, including the reporting of taxable benefits and the proper characterization of workers, and to ensure that GST/HST registry status and account balances are up to date. It is also used for assessing deficiencies, penalties, and interest when applicable, making copies when being examined, and to send requirement for information  notices. Personal information is collected under sections 152,153, 227 and 231 and subsection 220(2) of the Income Tax Act, section 200 of the Income Tax Regulations; section 288 and 296 and subsection 275(2) of the Excise Tax Act, sections 82, 85 and 88 of the Employment Insurance Act, section 11 of the Insurable Earnings and Collection of Premiums Regulations, sections 8, 9, 21, 22 and 25 of the Canada Pension Plan, and section 10 of the Canada Pension Plan Regulations .The Social Insurance Number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act, section 88 of the Canada Pension Plan Regulations, and section 89 of the Employment Insurance Regulations, and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation, including Department of Justice Canada (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services (JUS PPU 010)) and Statistics Canada (Memorandum of Understanding between Canada Revenue Agency and Statistics Canada to outline the roles and responsibilities for the release of income tax, GST and HST information). Information may also be disclosed to provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed information-sharing agreements, including Revenue Québec, and Nova Scotia Workers Compensation Board. Information may also be disclosed to internal CRA Programs including: Collections and Verification Business Intelligence, Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Canada Pension Plan and Employment Insurance Rulings Program (CRA PPU 070), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), GST/HST Audit and examination (CRA PPU 430), Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits (CRA PPU 140), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), Detection and Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Corporate Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220), Appeals Regarding the Canada Pension Plan and the Employment Insurance Act (CRA PPU 130), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571),  and Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as: the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance, data integrity, and training), responses to service complaints or other enquiries, compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics, and reporting. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated information analysis, information matching and knowledge discovery outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting, and workload development. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained electronically or at a private-sector records storage facility for 10 years and then are  destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA CVB 188 TBS Registration Number: 001948 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 120

### Excise and Specialty Tax
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the licensing, audit, compliance, and regulatory review of taxpayers under the non-GST/HST portions of the Excise Tax Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act, the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, and the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006. Personal information may include name, contact information, financial information, date of birth, biographical information, gender, language, business number, other identification numbers, criminal checks, user name and temporary password, and signature. Note : This PIB was updated in 2024 and was formerly entitled Excise Duties, Taxes and Special Levies Licencing, Registering and Compliance. Class of Individuals : Sole proprietorships, partners in a partnership, authorized officers, and contact persons. Purpose : Personal information is used to administer the excise and specialty tax program including assessing an applicant’s eligibility for the type of licence or registration required; identifying instances of non-compliance; and generation of mailing lists for technical materials. Personal information is collected under the authority of section 5 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act which gives to the CRA the mandate to support the administration and enforcement of the legislation under the responsibility of the Minister of National Revenue. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions, including: Department of Finance Canada (for fiscal policy purposes, FIN PPU 100), Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010), Canada Border Services Agency (Accounts Receivable Ledger, CBSA PPU 1501) and for the administration of Customs Act in regards to imports by licensees, Environment and Climate Change Canada in regards to the application of Part 2 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, Health Canada in regards to for the administration and enforcement of the Cannabis Act and the related amendments to the Excise Act, 2001. Information may also be shared with private sector, provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) for the administration and enforcement of related legislation to the extent authorized by law. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs, including: Commodity Tax Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 177), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), Accounts receivable (PSU 932), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223) and Specialty Business Returns (CRA PPU 224). Personal information may also be used for data-matching purposes, program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), strategy development, and reporting for other statistical analysis purposes. Retention and Disposal Standards: Active licensee, registrant and prescribed persons information is kept on an ongoing basis. Records on individuals who have ceased to be licensed or registered are maintained at a private-sector contractor records storage facility for a period of 10 years after which they are destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA LPRAB 030 TBS Registration: 003128 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 062

### Film and Media Tax Credit Program
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the verification of qualifying requirements of claims filed by corporations under the Film and Media Tax Credit Program, including the identification of non-compliance with the legislative requirements, tax laws, policies and procedures. Personal information may include: name, contact information, social insurance number (SIN), citizenship/residency status, biographical information, place of birth, date of birth, financial information and other identification numbers to verify citizenship or residency status (e.g. passport, driver’s licence, permanent resident card, etc.). Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the SIN, Business Number, and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This personal information bank was updated in 2025. Class of Individuals : Individuals hired by film and media tax credit claimant corporations for which salaries, wages and/or remuneration is being claimed as an eligible expense for the calculation of film and media tax credits, authorized corporate officers, contact persons, representatives and designated authorities, employees, external consultants, claim preparers. Purpose: Personal information is used for the following purposes: to verify citizenship or residency of individuals whose salaries are being claimed as labour expenses in the calculation of the various film and media tax credits to determine the eligibility of such expenses. It is also used to verify that the salaries paid to the individuals qualify as labour expenditures for the purposes of calculating the tax credits. It is collected under the authority of sections 125.4, 125.5, 162 and subsection 150 (1) of the Income Tax Act and sections 1106 and 9300 of the Income Tax Regulations. It is also collected pursuant to section 63(1) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act, which allows the CRA to enter into agreements with provincial government to administer a tax measure. The SIN is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Act and is used for identification purposes as well for the determination of the individual’s citizenship or residency status. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with income tax legislation, including the Department of Canadian Heritage (co-administration of the film and media tax credit program with the Canadian Audio-Visual Certification Office CAVCO). Information may also be disclosed to other applicable provincial/territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed information sharing agreements. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs/Activities, including: Income Tax and Charity Objections and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Corporate Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047) and Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity) and reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained for 10 years after the last administrative action and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA CPB 150 TBS Registration Number: 20170001 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 442

### Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits
Description: This bank describes personal information related to the determination of an individual's eligibility and entitlement for the goods and services tax / harmonized sales tax credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits: including the Alberta climate leadership adjustment rebate, the Newfoundland and Labrador income supplement, which includes the Newfoundland and Labrador disability amount and the Newfoundland and Labrador seniors benefit, the Newfoundland and Labrador disability benefit, the New Brunswick harmonized sales tax credit, the Prince Edward Island sales tax credit, the Saskatchewan low-income tax credit, the British Columbia harmonized sales tax credit, the Nova Scotia affordable living tax credit, the Nunavut carbon credit , the Ontario trillium benefit, which includes the Ontario sales tax credit, the Ontario energy and property tax credit and the Northern Ontario energy credit, the Ontario seniors homeowner property tax grant, the British Columbia climate action tax credit, the Yukon Government carbon price rebate – individuals, the Northwest Territories cost of living offset, the Canada Carbon Rebate (formerly known as the Climate action incentive payments), and the advanced Canada workers benefit. Personal information may include name, contact information, language, date of birth, date of death, place of birth, Social Insurance Number, other identification numbers, biographical information, employment information, financial information, gender, credit history, medical information, public trustee information, citizenship information, Internet Protocol Address (IP), attestation, and signature. Personal information may also include federal prison incarceration dates and opinions or views about the program. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide information to confirm authorization including the social insurance number. Information may be stored in CRA internal systems or databases that record all applicable financial transactions and the reasons why a payment may have been withheld. In addition, the databank records any subsequent activity on an individual's account (e.g., changed eligibility or entitlement and additional payment issued). This PIB was updated in 2025. Class of Individuals: Individual taxpayers, family members (dependents, spouses, common-law partners), authorized and legal representatives. Purpose : Personal information is used for the following purpose: to determine an individual's eligibility and entitlement for the GST/HST credit and related benefits and credits through processing, and validation activities. Validation activities may include authorized automated personal information analysis to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA; including the receipt of admission dates from Correctional Service Canada (CSC PPU 025) under the authority of subsection 8(2)(a) of the Privacy Act to determine continued eligibility. Personal information is also used for payment processing purposes and payment set-offs. The authority to perform a set-off is granted under subsection 164(2) of the Income Tax Act and subsection 155(1) of the Financial Administration Act. Personal information is collected under the authority of sections 122.5,122.8, and subsection 220(1) of the Income Tax Act. The social insurance number is collected under section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Section 61 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act allows the CRA to implement agreements with other federal, provincial and territorial governments for the purpose of carrying out an activity or program administered by the CRA. Consistent Uses : Information may be disclosed to an individual participating in the program or their representative or an authorized designated official described in subparagraphs 241 (4)(d)(ii) and 241(4)(d)(iii) of the Income Tax Act. Information may be shared and/or verified with other federal, provincial, or territorial government institutions in accordance with legislation and signed information-sharing agreements. This includes Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments PWGSC PCU 712) for the issuance of payments, the Department of Finance (Tax Data – Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy FIN PPU 100) and Statistics Canada for statistical analysis, and Employment and Social Development Canada for policy analysis, research, and evaluation. Information may be shared with third Iron Mountain and Nimble Inc. for secure storage purposes. Information may also be shared with third parties upon consent of the taxpayer for verification purposes. Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with legislation for the fiscal and policy analysis and program evaluation. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs/activities, including: Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Canada Child Benefit and Related Benefits (CRA PPU 063), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Individual Refund Set-Off Program (CRA PPU 213), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Canada workers benefit (CRA PPU 178), Scientific Research and Experimental Development (CRA PPU 441), National Security Disclosures (CRA PPU 202), Insider Risk Management (Guardian) Program (CRA PPU 921). Information may also be used or shared for other purposes such as: for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g., audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), audit, evaluation, and reporting and/or statistical purposes, compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, and reports. Risk management and processing activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow-through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be digitalized and retained at a private-sector contractor records storage facility for a period of ten years following the last administrative action and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority : 2017/12 Canada Revenue Agency and 2018/013 Destruction of Source Records following Digitization. Related Class of Record Number: CRA ABSB 648 TBS Registration: 003016 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 140

### GST/HST Audit and Examination
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the administration of CRA’s Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits compliance program including the administration of related provincial programs on behalf of those provinces. This may include collecting and/or verifying information from provincial and federal departments or agencies, private sector, and from public sources including newspapers and the internet. Personal information may include: name, contact information, gender, citizenship, language, credit history, date of birth, date of death, financial information, biographical information, medical information, criminal history, social insurance number, business number, other identification numbers such the pension plan number and diplomatic and consular card identification number, risk profiles, signature and internet protocol (IP) address. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the social insurance number, business number, and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This PIB was updated in 2023. Class of Individuals : Individuals, sole proprietors, partners in a partnership and other business owners -both registrants and non-registrants - who have filed returns, rebate applications, and/or elections related to the GST/HST returns and rebate program, their employees, corporate officers and/or representatives. Purpose : Personal information is used for reviews, examinations and audits at the domestic and international level to ensure compliance with Canada's tax laws. Personal information is collected and used for this purpose under the authority of the Excise Tax Act, sections 275(1), 288, 289, 289.1, 292, 295(5)(d)(i), and 295(5)(d)(ii), and the Income Tax Act, section 241(4)(d)(ii). The social insurance number is collected under the Social Insurance Number Disclosure Regulations, SOR/91-41 and section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses : Information may be used to verify compliance and identify risk factors.  Information may be shared with other federal government institutions, including: Department of Finance Canada (for fiscal policy purposes), Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010) and the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA PPU 1501). Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed memoranda of understanding. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs/Activities, including: Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Commodity Tax Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 177), Income Tax and Charity Objections and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Detection and Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035). Information may also be used or shared for other purposes such as: for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics and reports. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow-through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting. The social insurance number may be disclosed to other federal institutions for the purpose of administration and enforcement of the Income Tax Act in accordance with conditions provided in the written collaborative arrangements . Retention and Disposal Standards : Information included in an audit file, including files that are set up for potential action but never audited is retained for 5 years after the appeal process is resolved and then destroyed. All other information obtained from outside sources for risk assessment purposes that do not lead to an administrative action will be destroyed after 2 years. Paper copies of personal information are stored and retained for their retention period at a private-sector facility and after the retention period, the files will be destroyed by the contractor in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number : CRA CPB 476 TBS Registration Number: 20160020 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 430

### GST/HST Returns and Rebates processing program
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the administration of CRA’s Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits program including related provincial programs on behalf of some provinces. This includes verifying, validating, processing, assessing, reassessing, , and posting information filed through the GST/HST returns, and other related forms regarding rebates and/or elections. These include but are not limited to: GST/HST return (GST34), GST/HST and Quebec Sales Tax return (RC7200), General Application for Rebate of GST/HST (GST189), GST/HST New Housing Rebate Application for Owner-Built Houses (GST191), GST/HST New Residential Rental Property Rebate Application (GST524) and Election for GST/HST Reporting Period (GST20). Personal information may include: name, contact information, financial information, photo identification, signature, social insurance number, other identification numbers (such as business number, certificate of Indian status card registration number, and diplomatic or consular card identification number), biographical information, language, citizenship, date of birth, and internet protocol (IP) address. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request Form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide a SIN, a BN, and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. Information may be stored in the following CRA internal business suite of systems: GST/HST Return and Adjustment Processing System, Rebates Processing System, Program Account Information System, Standardized Accounting system, Business Number system, and Business Client Communication System. This personal information bank was updated in 2025. Class of individuals : Individuals, sole proprietors, other business owners, officers of a corporation, and/or their representatives - both registrants and non-registrants - who have filed returns, rebates, and/or elections related to GST/HST. Purpose: Personal information is collected under the authority of Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, section 7 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act, sub-section 63(1) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act, Title I of the Act Respecting the Quebec Sales Tax (chapter t-0.1 ), and the Tax Administration Act (Quebec), and is used to administer and enforce the GST/HST program, including processing, assessing, validating, reassessing, and verifying GST/HST returns, various rebates, and elections, as well as administering the Quebec Sales Tax. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to Part IX of the Excise Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be used for the payment of debts owed to the Crown. Where applicable, information pertaining to an individual’s indebtedness may be shared with the individual’s debtors and the courts to facilitate attachment. Information may be shared and/or verified on a case-by-case basis with third parties to identify or clarify missing information on GST/HST returns, rebate applications and elections. Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed information sharing agreements. Information may also be shared with other federal institutions in accordance with legislation including: Global Affairs Canada (Members of the Diplomatic Corps in Canada, GAC PPU 907); Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data - Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy, FIN PPU 100); Statistics Canada, (Classes of Personal Information  - Business survey); Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments, PWGSC PCU 712); Crown Indigenous Relations and Northern Affairs Canada (CIRNAC) (No PIB), and Canada Border Services Agency (Administrative Monetary Penalty System Program, CBSA PPU 001). Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs/activities, including: GST/HST Rulings and Interpretations (CRA PPU 092); Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047); Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223); Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005); Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025); Collections (CRA PPU 050); Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120); Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421); Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220); Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172); Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571); Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580); Commodity Tax Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 177); International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035); GST/HST Audit and Examination (CRA PPU 430); National Security Disclosures (PIB pending); Insider Risk Management (Guardian) Program (CRA PPU 921); and Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095). Information may also be used or shared for other purposes such as: for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g., audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics, and reports. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records are retained for 10 years and then destroyed. Paper copies are stored at a private-sector records storage facility after processing. Record Disposition Authority : 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA ABSB 246 TBS Registration Number: 000013 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 241

### GST/HST Rulings and Interpretations
Description: This bank describes personal information related to the correspondence received from GST/HST registrants or claimants, softwood lumber products export charge registrants, Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act registrants, excise tax and excise duty licensees, and other persons requesting an interpretation of a section, subsection, etc., of the Excise Tax Act, Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006, Excise Act, Excise Act, 2001, Air Travellers Security Charge Act, Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act, and related Regulations, and the replies sent by the CRA. Personal information may include: name, contact information, financial information, gender, language, signature, and business number. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide proof of identification including name, address, licence or registration number, and where applicable written third-party authorization. Information may be stored in the following CRA internal systems or databases: Rulings and Interpretations Tracking System. This PIB was updated in 2024. Class of Individuals: GST/HST registrants and claimants, air travellers security charge registrants, softwood lumber products export charge registrants, Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act registrants, excise duty licensees and registrants, excise tax licensees, and their authorized representatives. Purpose: The information is used to provide responses that address situations or issues on matters related to the Acts and Regulations. Personal information is collected under the authority of the Excise Tax Act, Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006, Excise Act, Excise Act, 2001, Air Travellers Security Charge Act, Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act and the related Regulations. Consistent Uses: Personal information provided in relation to a request may be accessed during research and analysis in the resolution of cases dealing with similar technical issues or in the preparation of other technical interpretations, rulings and publications. Information may be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs/Activities, including: Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits (CRA PPU 140), Commodity Tax Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 177), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035) and Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220). If applicable, information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts e.g., program evaluation (quality assurance), compliance, data matching, enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics and reports. Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with section 295 of the Excise Tax Act, section 211of the Excise Act, 2001, Air Travellers Security Charge Act, and section 107 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, section 84 of the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006 including: Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data – Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy FIN PPU 100), Department of the Environment (relating to Part 2 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act or for the formulation or evaluation of policies) and Department of Justice Canada (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services JUS PPU 010). Information may also be disclosed to other applicable provincial/territorial governments in accordance with signed taxpayer information sharing agreements. Retention and Disposal Standards: Rulings and interpretations are retained at a private-sector contractor records storage facility for 20 years and then transferred to Library and Archives Canada for historical purposes. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA LPRAB 140 TBS Registration Number: 003777 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 092
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the unauthorized third-party use of taxpayer information with the Canada Revenue Agency. Personal information may include:  social insurance number, biographical information, contact information, date of birth, date of death, financial information, gender, language, name, other identification numbers (including driver’s licence, passport, health cards and status cards). Note :  In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide information to confirm authorization, including the social insurance number. This personal information bank was created in 2024. Class of Individuals : Individual taxpayers, including non-residents of Canada, and their family members (dependents, spouses, common-law partners). Also includes representatives and/or delegated authorities. Purpose : Personal information is used for the following purpose: to conduct taxpayer verification to ensure compliance withthe filing requirements of individuals as outlined in the Income Tax Act (ITA). Personal information is collected under the authority of the Income Tax Act, the Rental Housing Benefit Act, the Dental Benefit Act, the Canada Revenue Agency Act, the Canada Recovery Benefit Act, and the delegation instrument issued pursuant to section 11 of the Department of Employment and Social Development Act to the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) in respect of the Canada Recovery Benefit Act, signed October 2, 2020, the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act, and the delegation instrument issued pursuant to section 11 of the Department of Employment and Social Development Act to the CRA in respect of the Canada Emergency Response Benefit Act, signed April 3, 2020, the Canada Emergency Student Benefit Act, and the delegation instrument issued pursuant to section 11 of the Department of Employment and Social Development Act to the CRA in respect of the Canada Emergency Student Benefit Act, signed May 12, 2020, the Canada Workers Lockdown Benefit Act, and the delegation instrument issued pursuant to section 11 of the Department of Employment and Social Development Act to the CRA in respect of the Canada Workers Lockdown Benefit Act, signed December 2, 2021. ### Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the resolution of objections and appeals to income tax and charities assessments, reassessments, and determinations. Personal information may include: name, biographical information, contact information, citizenship status, residency status, credit information, date of birth, date of death, marital status, employee personnel information, financial information, language, medical information, tax account/filing information, opinion or views of, or about, individuals, signature, employment status, social insurance number, business number, and other identification numbers. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide: the subject and date of correspondence, social insurance number, legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate, and business number. This personal information bank was updated in 2024. Class of Individuals : Individual taxpayers including non-residents, sole proprietors, partners, other business owners and their employees, shareholders, corporate officers, representatives and designated authorities, agents, brokers, insurers, those insured, and nominees who will file an objection or an appeal to the courts of Canada related to assessments and/or reassessments of Income Tax, Canada Child Benefits determinations, and charities status. Purpose : Personal information is collected under the Authority of sections  220 and 241of the Income Tax Act, and sections 81.15 and 275 of the Excise Tax Act and is used for the following purpose: to resolve income tax and charity objections and appeals; determine eligibility for the GST/HST credit, Canada Child benefit, and Canada workers benefit. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and Part IX of the Excise Tax Act is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government departments in accordance with legislation, including the Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010), federal courts of Canada, and Department of Finance (Tax Data – Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy, FIN PPU 100). Information may also be disclosed to Revenu Québec, foreign governments for which there is a Tax Treaty, and with provincial/territorial governments. Information may also be used or disclosed to third parties named in a civil proceeding (see subsection 241(4) of the Income Tax Act). Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs/activities, and local offices involved in the objections and appeals such as: Collections (CRA PPU 050), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), T3 Assessment program (CRA PPU 015), Tax-free Savings Initiatives (CRA PPU 054), Specialty Business Returns (CRA PPU 224), GST/HST Returns and Rebates Program (CRA PPU 241), Canada Child Benefit and Related Benefits (CRA PPU 063), Disability Tax Credit (DTC) (CRA PPU 218), Working Income Tax Benefit (CRA PPU 178), Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits (CRA PPU 140), and Underused Housing Tax (CRA PPU 214). Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g., audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, data matching, strategy development, statistics and reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards : Case file records are retained for ten years after the appeal period with regard to the decision on a case made in the highest court has been exhausted. Records are then destroyed. When cases are heard before a court, such as the Tax Court of Canada, case files and records produced by the CRA for the court are retained for two years after the file closes and transferred to Library and Archives Canada. ### Individual Returns and Payment Processing
Description : This bank describes personal information that is used for the administration and enforcement of federal and provincial income tax legislation including the verification, validation, processing, and assessment of tax-related and benefit-related information received from individual taxpayers, including non-residents , sole proprietors and partners in partnerships, as well as from third party sources, including representatives of employers, financial institutions, trust companies and government institutions. This bank also describes information concerning taxpayer payment processing options, including pre-authorized debit. Personal information may include: name, contact information, credit history, biographical information, citizenship status, date of birth, date of death, financial information, medical information, incarceration dates, other identification numbers (business number, individual tax number, temporary taxation number, EFILER number, trustee individual number, old age security number, tax/pension account number), signature, social insurance number, filing information, internet protocol(IP) address, consent for organ and tissue donor contact, consent for Elections Canada contact, and language. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the social insurance number, temporary taxation number, individual tax number, and legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This personal information bank was updated in 2023. Class of Individuals : Individual taxpayers including non-residents ; family members (dependants, spouses, common-law partners), sole proprietors, partners in partnerships, representatives, individuals who act as contacts for employers, financial institutions, trust companies and trustees on behalf of an individual who is in bankruptcy. It also includes individuals eligible for veterans affairs disability programs. Purpose : The personal information is collected under the authority of sections 150, 220 and 241 of the Income Tax Act, and section 7 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act; and is used to process individual taxpayer income tax returns, including initial assessments, payment processing, validations, accounting, and adjustments for the federal government and for most provinces and territories, including determining eligibility for various tax credits, benefits and rebates; including COVID-19 relief measures governed by legislative authority. Validation activities may include authorized automated personal information analysis to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA; including the receipt of eligible individuals from Veterans Affairs Canada (VAC MVA 820) under the authority of 241(4)(h.1) of the Income Tax Act to determine eligibility for the COVID-19 one-time payment for persons with disabilities. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Information is also collected under subsection 92(2) of the Canada Pension Plan and Parts I, IV and VII.1 of the Employment Insurance Act, self-employed individuals are required to file a return if subject to Canada Pension Plan contributions and/or Employment Insurance premiums on self-employed earnings.  Under the section 46.1 of the Canada Elections Act, we also collect individual’s consent for Elections Canada to add the individual to the registered voters list. Personal information is shared and received as section 61 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act allows the Agency to enter into contracts, agreements or other arrangements with governments, public or private organizations and agencies or any person in the name of His Majesty in right of Canada or in its own name for the purposes of the above activities. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation, including Statistics Canada (Longitudinal Administrative Databank StatCan PPU 112,  Longitudinal Immigration Database StatCan PPU 135 and Census of Population and National Household survey StatCan PPU 005), Department of Justice Canada (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services JUS PPU 010, Garnishment Registry JUS PPU 150 Family Orders and Agreements Enforcement Assistance JUS PPU 125), Department of Finance Canada (Fiscal Policy Analysis FIN PPU 100), Canada Border Services Agency (Receivable Ledger PPU 1501), Employment and Social Development Canada (administration of a one-time payment to persons with disabilities for reasons related to COVID-19 relief measures, Social Insurance Number Register ESDC PPU 390, Old Age Security Program ESDC PPU 116, Employment Insurance Claim Files ESDC PPU 151, Benefit Overpayment File ESDC PPU 180, and for the administration of the Canadian Dental Care Plan by ESDC on behalf of Health Canada Personal Information Bank Pending), Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada (Net Income Stabilization Account AAFC PPU 183), Elections Canada (Voter Registration and Identification Elections PPU 037), Veterans Affairs Canada (Earnings Loss VAC PPU 607), Canadian Forces (Income Supplement VAC PPU 608), Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (Application and Assessment for Canadian Citizenship IRCC PPU 050) and with Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments PWGSC PCU 712). Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed information sharing agreements and Information may also be shared with third parties upon consent of the taxpayer for income verification purposes. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs including: Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223), Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and Related Benefits and Credits (CRA PPU 140), Canada Child Benefit and Related Benefits (CRA PPU 063), Working Income Tax Benefit (CRA PPU 178), Disability Tax Credit Program (CRA PPU 218), Children’s Special Allowances (CRA PPU 620), Tax-Free Savings Initiatives (CRA PPU 054), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Income Tax and Charity Objections and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Appeals regarding the Canada Pension Plan and the Employment Insurance Act (CRA PPU 130), Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120), Part XIII Non-Resident Withholding Program (CRA PPU 094), Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085), Charities Program (CRA PPU 200) Canada Pension Plan and Employment Insurance Rulings Program (CRA PPU 070), Registered Deferred Income and Savings Plan (CRA PPU 226), Advance Income Tax Rulings and Technical Interpretations (CRA PPU 090), Remissions (CRA PPU 142), Individual Refund Set-Off Program (CRA PPU 213), Monitoring of Electronic Access to Taxpayer Information (CRA PPU 718) and Authentication and Credential Management Service (CRA PPU 607), One-time top-up to the Canada Housing Benefit (CRA PPU 621), Canada Dental Benefit (CRA PPU 644). Where applicable, information pertaining to an individual’s indebtedness may be shared with the tax filer’s debtors and the courts to facilitate attachment. Lastly, information may also be used or disclosed for CRA corporate purposes including the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, business intelligence, analytics, and may be used for data-matching purposes, reporting and for survey or other statistical analysis purposes. Retention and Disposal Standards : The retention period is of 10 years after activity is complete, and then destroyed. Income verification request files are retained for two years and response files are kept for 180 days on tape by the Information and Technology Branch, at which point they are purged, and reused as necessary.

### Individual Refund Set-Off Program
Description : This bank describes personal information that is used to process set-offs under the Refund Set-off program, including information received from the taxpayer and from other federal, provincial and territorial government institutions. Personal information may include: name, date of birth, biographical information, gender, contact information, financial information, other identification numbers, and the social insurance number. Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed information sharing agreements. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs/Activities, including: Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits (CRA PPU 140), Working Income Tax Benefit (CRA PPU 178), Tax-Free Savings Initiatives (CRA PPU 054), EFILE Online Services (CRA PPU 211), Discounters Program (CRA PPU 212), Monitoring of Electronic Access to Taxpayer Information (CRA PPU 718) and Collections (CRA PPU 050). Lastly, information may also be used or disclosed for CRA corporate purposes including the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g., audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, may be used for data-matching purposes and reporting.
