### Insider Risk Management (Guardian) Program
Description: This bank describes personal information used for the purposes of the Canada Revenue Agency’s (CRA) continuous assurance activities in identifying and mitigating insider risks. This is accomplished by leveraging existing CRA capabilities, information and assisting in the development of new security controls and processes. Insider risks include anyone with access to specific resources, networks, systems, and operations and facilities, such as current or former employees, business partners, or contractors. Personal information may include: name, CRA user ID, date of birth, citizenship status, credit history, personal record identifier (PRI) and employee personnel information. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide: PRI. This PIB was created in 2022 for the Phase I roll-out and will be updated as program progression occurs. Class of Individuals : Employees of the Agency, and other individuals using the Agency’s information systems, including: students, contract staff and agency personnel. Purpose : Personal information is used for the purposes of continuous assurance in order to identify and mitigate insider risks. Personal information is collected under the authority of subsections 30(1) and 51(1) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with internal CRA programs, including: Personnel Security Screening Program (Personnel Security Screening – CRA PPU 917, Personnel Security Screening PSU 917), Security Risk Assessment Program (Security Incidents and Privacy Breaches PSU 939, Physical Access Controls PSU 907), Disclosure of Wrongdoing in the Workplace PSU 906), Internal Affairs and Fraud Control Program (Monitoring of Electronic Access to Taxpayer Information CRA PPU 718, Authentication and Credential Management Service CRA PPU 607, CRA Internal Identity and Credential Management Services CRA PPU 165, Electronic Network Monitoring Logs PSU 905), Cyber Security Program (Authentication and Credential Management Service CRA PPU 607, CRA Internal Identity and Credential Management Services CRA PPU 165, Electronic Network Monitoring Logs PSU 905), IT Security and Continuity Program (Authentication and Credential Management Service CRA PPU 607, CRA Internal Identity and Credential Management Services CRA PPU 165, Electronic Network Monitoring Logs PSU 905, Information Security Program (Authentication and Credential Management Service CRA PPU 607, CRA Internal Identity and Credential Management Services CRA PPU 165, Electronic Network Monitoring Logs PSU 905), National Human Resources Program (Human Resources Planning PSU 935, Attendance and Leave PSE 903, Training and Development PSE 905, Occupational Health and Safety PSE 907), Labour Relations Program (Canadian Human Rights Act – Complaints PSU 933, Discipline PSE 911, Grievances PSE 910, Harassment PSE 919, Disclosure of Wrongdoing in the Workplace PSU 906, Values and Ethics Codes for the Public Sector and Organizational Code(s) of Conduct PSE 915). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as: research, planning, audit and evaluation, and reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards : Records are retained for 2 years after the program file/case is closed and then are destroyed. Training, education and awareness information is retained for 2 years after the completion of training and then are destroyed. ### International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs
Description: This bank describes personal information that is used for verification of compliance and the administration and enforcement of provisions under the Income Tax Act for International and Large Business (ILB), and High Net Worth (HNW) Compliance Tax Audit Programs, and taxpayers who have cross-border transactions with related parties or who have foreign affiliates or issues involving international transactions of unreported foreign income and undisclosed assets. Personal information is received from corporations, partnerships, trusts and individuals, and includes, but is not limited to information received through the Reportable Transaction Information returns (RC312), Reportable Uncertain Tax Treatments Information returns (RC3133), Foreign Information returns (T106, T1134, T1135, T1141, and T1142), Tax Shelter forms (T5001, T5003, and T5004), Electronic Funds Transfers by financial entities or money service businesses, Country-by-Country reports, and various other forms and elections. Personal information may also be collected during a CRA audit and indirectly from sources other than the taxpayer. This may include collecting and/or verifying information from third party data sources including federal, provincial, and territorial public bodies, private sector organizations, and from publicly available open sources including print, broadcast, and digital media. Personal information may include: name, contact information, citizenship status, language, date of birth, date of death, credit history, financial information, biographical information, signature, social insurance number, and other identification numbers (business number, tax identification number, birth certificate, passport number, permanent resident card number, provincial health card number, driver’s license and other provincial license numbers). Personal information belonging to CRA employees, such as employee identification numbers and employee personnel information, may also be collected to support the administration, operation, and monitoring of audit programs. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the social insurance number, tax identification number and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This personal information bank was updated in 2026. Class of Individuals: Individual taxpayers, including non-residents, family members (dependents, spouses, common-law partners), sole proprietors, partners in a partnership, corporate officers, employees and persons under examination, shareholders, representatives and designated authorities, agents, trustees, settlors, beneficiaries, tax shelter promoters and advisors, tax informants, and CRA employees. Purpose: The personal information is used to administer International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs, including all related compliance activities. Personal information is collected pursuant to subsections 85(1), 85(2), 97(2), and 220(1) of the Income Tax Act, sections 163.2, 231.1, 231.2, 231.4, 231.5, 231.6, 231.7, 233.1 to 233.6233.8, 237.1, 237.3, 237.4, 237.5, 244.2, 263 to 268, and 270 to 281 of the Income Tax Act, section 285.1 of the Excise Tax Act, and section 61 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Personal information belonging to CRA employees is handled pursuant to subsections 6(2) and 30(1)(d) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act, section 241(4) of the Income Tax Act, section 295(5) of the Excise Tax Act and section 211(6) of the Excise Act 2001. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with income tax legislation, including Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data - Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy, FIN PPU 100); Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010); Public Prosecution Service of Canada (Prosecutions and Prosecution-related Activities, PPSC PPU 002); Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada (Financial Analysis and Disclosures, FINTRAC PPU 020); Office of the Auditor General of Canada (Performance Audit Files, OAG PPU 002); and Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (Notices of directors of corporations, ISED PPU 049). Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed information sharing agreements to the extent authorized by law. Personal information obtained about residents of countries with which Canada has Income Tax Conventions may be shared with those taxing authorities by way of an exchange of information under the appropriate treaty provision. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs, including: Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005); Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047); Advance Income Tax Rulings and Technical Interpretations (CRA PPU 090); Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223); Charities Program (CRA PPU 200); Collections (CRA PPU 050); Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085); GST/HST Audit and Examination (CRA PPU 430); Scientific Research and Experimental Development (CRA PPU 441); Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172); Commodity Tax Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 177); Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571); Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421); T3 Assessment program (CRA PPU 015); Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580); Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095); Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025); Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220); Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120); Offshore Tax Informant Program (CRA PPU 411); and Monitoring of Electronic Access to Taxpayer Information (CRA PPU 718). Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts; program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity); compliance and enforcement risk management; strategy development; statistics, and reporting. Risk management activities may include automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, including business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliance. Retention and Disposal Standards : Audit files are retained for ten years after the appeal process is resolved and then destroyed. Records related to valuations are retained for seven years and then are destroyed. Data received from financial institutions related to electronic funds transfers is retained for 10 years and then destroyed. Paper records are stored and retained at a private-sector contractor records storage facility. All other information obtained from outside sources for risk assessment purposes that do not lead to an administrative action will be destroyed after two years. ### Monitoring of electronic access to taxpayer information
Description: This bank describes personal information used to monitor employee accesses to taxpayer information (and other similar information) to ensure that all accesses were in accordance with their assigned workload and duties. Personal information may include: name, date of birth, contact information, user ID, biographical information, personal record identifier, employee personnel information, social insurance number, business number and other program account numbers. Screen captures may also include additional taxpayer and other similar information such as, but not limited to, citizenship status, credit information, date of death, financial information, medical information and gender. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide: social insurance number, business number, other program account numbers and/or personal record identifier. Information is stored in the following CRA internal system(s) or database(s): Enterprise Fraud Management. This personal information bank was updated in 2019. Class of Individuals: Employees of the Agency, and other individuals using the Agency’s information systems, including: students, contract staff and agency personnel; taxpayers whose information is recorded in audit trails or captured transactions. Purpose: Personal information is used to detect and proactively identify questionable transactions of employees viewing and manipulating taxpayer and other sensitive information through the application of business intelligence and is collected under the authority of Paragraph 30(1)(a)(d) and Section 51(1)(f),(g) and (i) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act; Sections 220, and 241(1) of the Income Tax Act; Sections 275 and 295(2) of the Excise Tax Act; Sections 7, 8 and 9 of the Excise Act; Sections 19 and 84(2) of the Softwood Lumber Products Exports Charge Act; and Sections 10(1) and 11 of the Children’s Special Allowances Act. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with the employee’s immediate supervisor or manager to review if the transactions were in accordance with the employee’s assigned workload and duties. The information may be used to conduct internal investigations if there are questionable transactions. Refer to bank Security Incidents and Privacy Breaches - PSU 939 for details. Information may also be used to substantiate any disciplinary action taken where violation of the Agency’s Policies is determined. Refer to bank Discipline – PSE 911 for details. Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as: research, planning, audit and evaluation, and reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained for the current fiscal year, plus four years after which they are destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: PRN 931 TBS Registration number: 20180024 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 718

### National Security Disclosures
Description: This bank describes personal information related to disclosures of taxpayer and/or confidential information to Government of Canada institutions listed in Schedule 3 of the Security of Canada Information Disclosure Act, when the information would be relevant to a national security investigation (as defined in the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act) and/or an investigation of a terrorism offence (as defined in the Criminal Code). Personal information may include but is not limited to: name, date of birth, date of death, social insurance number, contact information, financial information, business numbers, incorporation information, opinions or views of, or about, individuals, biographical information, criminal checks / history, citizenship and residency status. Note: This bank was created in 2025. Class of Individuals: Individual taxpayers including non-residents; family members (dependants, spouses, common-law partners), sole proprietors, partners in partnerships, other business owners, corporate officers, shareholders, representatives and designated authorities, agents, trustees, settlors, and beneficiaries. Purpose: To administer the disclosure of taxpayer and/or confidential information to recipient government institutions listed in Schedule 3 of the Security of Canada Information Disclosure Act, when the information is deemed relevant to investigations relating to threats to the security of Canada. Personal information is used to determine if the statutory thresholds for disclosure of taxpayer and/or confidential information have been met and document requests received from recipient government institutions. Personal information is collected under the authority of subsections 241 (9) of the Income Tax Act, 295 (5.05) of the Excise Tax Act, and 211 (6.5) of the Excise Act, 2001, which set out the CRA’s legal authorities for the disclosure of taxpayer and/or confidential information to a recipient government institution listed in Schedule 3 of the Security of Canada Information Disclosure Act. Taxpayer and/or confidential information as defined under subsections 241 (10) of the Income Tax Act, and subsections 295 (1) of the Excise Tax Act, and 211 (1) of the Excise Act, 2001, can be disclosed when there are reasonable grounds to suspect that the information would be relevant to: (i) an investigation of whether the activity of any person may constitute threats to the security of Canada, as defined in section 2 of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act, or (ii) an investigation of whether any of the following offences may have been committed: a terrorism offence as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code; and, an offence under section 462.31 of the Criminal Code, if that investigation is related to a terrorism offence as defined in section 2 of that Act. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with recipient government institutions   listed in Schedule 3 of the Security of Canada Information Disclosure Act to assist them in meeting their respective national security mandates. Such institutions include the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP PPU 025) and Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS PPU 045). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), and program evaluation. The social insurance number may be disclosed to recipient government institutions for the purpose of identifying individuals with certainty. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained for 10 years after the program file is closed, at which stage the file would then be destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA LPRAB 292 TBS Registration Number: 20250044 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 202

### Non-Filer Compliance
Description: This bank describes personal information related to non-filers ’ (individuals, businesses, corporations, and trusts) income tax and information returns to assist in investigations, and other compliance actions taken under the Income Tax Act (ITA) to ensure compliance where all individuals, businesses, corporations and trusts file the required returns, and to analyze and identify compliance trends, emerging issues and the factors underlying non-compliance behaviour. This may include collecting and/or verifying information from provincial, territorial and federal institutions, the private sector, and from public sources including the internet. Personal information may include: name, biographical information, citizenship status, credit history, financial information, gender, social insurance number (SIN), contact information, language, other identification numbers (business number (BN), driver’s licence), date of death and date of birth, work history, curriculum vitae, family information, hobbies and interests. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the SIN, BN, date of birth, and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. Information may be stored in the following CRA internal systems or databases: System Universal Delpac System (SUDS) and the Automated Trust System (ATS). This PIB was updated in 2025, and modifications are pending TBS approval. Class of Individuals: Individual taxpayers including non-residents , their family members (dependents, spouses, common-law partners), sole proprietors, partners in a partnership, other business owners, shareholders, corporate officers, representatives, designated authorities and trustees. Purpose: Personal information is used for the following purpose: To detect, identify, and effectively address instances of non-compliance , and to encourage voluntary compliance. Personal information is collected under the authority of paragraph 150(1)e, section 231, and subsections 150(2), 152(7), 220(1), 220(2), 231.1, 231.2(1), 231.7(1), 233(1), and 233(2)of the ITA, sections 2 and 61 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act, and Section 2 of the Criminal Code. The SIN is collected pursuant to subsections 237(1.1), 237(1.2), 237(2), 237(3) and 237(4) of the ITA. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation and/or signed information-sharing agreements, including: Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010), Public Prosecution Service of Canada (Prosecutions and Prosecution-related Activities, PPSC PPU 002) and Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (Inquiries and Compliance Activities, ISED PPU 050). Information may also be disclosed to applicable provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof), in accordance with legislation and signed information- sharing agreements. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs including: Specialty Business Returns (CRA PPU 224), Canada Child Benefits (CRA PPU 063), GST/HST Returns and Rebates Program (CRA PPU 241), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223), Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Advance Income Tax Rulings  Technical Interpretations (CRA PPU 090), Collections (CRA PPU 050), GST / HST Audit and Examination (CRA PPU 430), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), T3 Assessment program (CRA PPU 015) and Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics, and reporting. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliance and workload development. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained at a private sector records storage facility for 10 years after a case is closed or after expiration of all appeal periods and then are destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA CVB 181 TBS Registration Number: 003542 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 025

### Office of the Taxpayers' Ombudsperson
Description: This bank describes personal information related to taxpayers' complaints to the Office of the Taxpayers' Ombudsperson, regarding the service they received from the Canada Revenue Agency. Personal information may include: name, contact information, signature, language, and complainants’ CRA Service complaint file. Note : This Personal Information Bank was updated in 2025. Class of Individuals: Individuals, businesses, corporations, charities, trusts, estates, persons who pay taxes or persons who are exempt from taxes, persons eligible to receive an amount as a benefit, or receive a service from the CRA, and their representatives.
Purpose: Personal information is collected under the authority of Order in council P.C. 2020-0703 and is used to confirm identity of the complainant, process complaints, to conduct independent and impartial examinations of the complaints, and to provide recommendations for resolution and improved service to taxpayers. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared, as applicable, with the CRA Ombudsperson Liaison Office (Service Feedback Program CRA PPU 571), as well as Ministerial correspondence officials. Information may also be used for the following purposes: follow-up, creating an inventory of complaints, program evaluation, and to identify and analyze systemic service-related issues and trends. Aggregate information is published in the Taxpayers' Ombudsperson's Annual Report to Parliament. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained for 2 years following the last administrative action and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA OTO 330 TBS Registration Number: 20090527 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 222

### Offshore Tax Informant Program
Description : This bank describes personal information related to CRA’s Offshore Tax Informant Program, which allows the CRA to give financial awards to individuals who provide information related to major international tax non-compliance that leads to the collection of taxes owing. Personal information may include name; contact information; date of birth; biographical information (work history, family information); financial information (including details provided to CRA by tax informants); social insurance number; other identification numbers (including business number, partnership number, trust account number, passport and driver’s licence); opinion or views of, or about, individuals; and signature. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form, individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide their social insurance number, business number and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This personal information bank was updated in 2024. Class of Individuals : Individual taxpayers who engage in offshore financial activities; their family members (dependents, spouses, common-law partners) and associates; authorized representatives, signing officers of a corporation, trust or of a partnership; and tax informants. Purpose : Personal information is collected under the authority of the Income Tax Act and the Excise Tax Act and is used to administer the Offshore Tax Informant Program. The social insurance number is used for identification purposes pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act. Consistent Uses: Personal information may be used and disclosed in relation to the administration or enforcement of the Income Tax Act or the Excise Tax Act including the following programs within CRA: Leads Program (CRA PPU 423); GST/HST Audit and Examination (CRA PPU 430); Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421); Detection and Investigations (CRA PPU 095); International and Large Business and High Net Worth Income Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035); Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025); and the Competent Authority Program (CRA PPU 085). Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics and reporting. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis; personal information matching; knowledge discovery techniques; to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA; to verify flow through reporting; business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards : Records related to the Offshore Tax Informant program are retained for ten years after a file is closed and are then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority Number : 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number : CRA CPB 415 TBS Registration : 20180023 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 411

### One-time top-up to the Canada Housing
Description : This bank describes personal information related to individuals who apply for the One-time top-up to the Canada Housing Benefit including their dependants, spouse or common-law partner, and authorized representative. Personal information may include: name, contact information, language, social insurance number, date of birth, date of death, employment information, financial information, rental information, biographical information, other processing information related to the application, internet protocol address and attestation to the eligibility requirements on the program. Note :  In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide information to confirm authorization, including the social insurance number. Information may be stored in CRA internal systems or databases which record all applicable financial transactions and the reasons why a payment may have been withheld. In addition, the databank records any subsequent activity on an individual's housing benefit account (e.g. changed eligibility or entitlement and additional payment issued). This Personal Information Bank (PIB) was reviewed in 2023. Class of Individuals : Individuals, or representatives, who apply for the One-time top-up to the Canada Housing Benefit, if applicable a spouse or common-law partner, and their dependents. Purpose : Personal information is used for the following purpose: to determine an individual's eligibility and entitlement for the One-time top-up to the Canada Housing Benefit through processing and validation activities. Validation activities may include authorized automated or manual personal information analysis to verify the accuracy of the information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to maintain the integrity of child and family benefits and credits programs. Personal information is also used for payment processing purposes and also to undertake measures to recoup funds that should not have been paid. Personal information is collected under the authority of the Rental Housing Benefit Act and section 241(4)(d)(xxi) of the Income Tax Act. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 13 of the Rental Housing Benefit Act and is used for identification purposes. Section 61 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act allows the CRA to implement agreements with other federal, provincial, and territorial governments for the purpose of carrying out an activity or program administered by the CRA. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation and signed information-sharing agreements, including Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments PWGSC PCU 712) for the issuance of payments. Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data – Evaluation and Formulation of fiscal Policy FIN PPU 100) and Statistics Canada for statistical analysis. Information may also be shared with third parties, such as the applicant’s landlord, for verification purposes. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs, including Collections (CRA PPU 050), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120), Public Communications (CRA PSU 914), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Leads Program (CRA PPU 423) and Individual Refund Set-Off Program (CRA PPU 213). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and statistical analysis), strategy development, and reports. Retention and Disposal Standards : Paper records will be retained at a private-sector records storage facility for a period of six years following the last administrative action and then destroyed. Electronic records will be retained for a period of six years following the last administrative action and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority : 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number :  CRA ABSB 626 TBS Registration Number :  20230027 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 621

### Part XIII Non-Resident Withholding Program
Description: This bank describes personal information related to the withholding, remitting and reporting obligations of Part XIII tax. Part XIII of the Income Tax Act requires tax to be withheld on certain types of passive or investment income paid to non-residents by Canadian payers (e.g. pension payments, rental income, dividends). Personal information may include: biographical information, name, contact information, language, social insurance number, other identification numbers (e.g. non-resident account number, individual tax number, business number, and trust number), date of birth, financial information, and signature. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form, individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide their account number, as well as the full name and address on the account. This personal information bank was updated in 2025. Class of Individuals: Canadians who pay income taxable under Part XIII of the Income Tax Act, non-resident beneficiaries of that income, their spouse or common-law partner, their dependents, and their authorized representative. Purpose: Personal information is used to administer the Part XIII Non-Resident Withholding Program including elections and undertakings. Personal information is collected under the authority of Part XIII, sections 212 through 218.1, of the Income Tax Act. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 and subsection 220(1) of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation and/or information-sharing agreements, including: Employment and Social Development Canada (Old Age Security  ESDC PPU 116, and Canada Pension Plan Program ESDC PPU 146), and Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver general payments PWGSC PCU 712). Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) to the extent authorized by law. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs, including: Collections (CRA PPU 050), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Commodity Tax Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 177), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), T3 Assessment Program (CRA PPU 015), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223), Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), GST/HST Returns and Rebates processing program (CRA PPU 241), Specialty Business Returns (CRA PPU 224), Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220) and Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580). Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance, data integrity, and training), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics and reporting. Risk management activities may include: any automated personal information analysis; personal information matching; knowledge discovery techniques; verifying the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA; verifying flow through reporting; business intelligence; and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliance. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained for ten years (depending on the type of information) at a private-sector contractor records storage facility. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA CVB 189 TBS Registration Number: 004464 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 094

### Personnel Security Screening - CRA
Description: This bank describes information that is related to security screening assessments of individuals working or applying for work with the Canada Revenue Agency. Personal information may include name(s), contact information, biographical information, biometric information (e.g., fingerprints, digital photographs, etc.), character assessments (e.g., loyalty, trustworthiness, etc.) citizenship status, credit information, criminal checks/history, law enforcement record checks, criminal associations, open-source intelligence, date of birth, gender, educational information, employee identification number, employee personnel information, financial information including tax enforcement and compliance information, other identification numbers, opinions and views of, or about, individuals, physical attributes, place of birth, signature, and military service information. The bank may also describe personal information about any immediate relatives, including name, contact information, date of birth and death, and relationship to applicant. Note: The CRA has discontinued the Reliability Status Plus (RS+) security screening level effective January 1, 2018 , and has adopted the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat (TBS) Security Screening Standard . As such, this personal information bank was deregistered in 2023 and the personal information is now described in the TBS standard PIB entitled Personnel Security Screening (PSU 917) . Class of Individuals: General public, including volunteers, all current and former employees, contractors, immediate relatives, current and former spouse/common law partner, agency, casual employees, and students, individuals who give character references (including neighbours), current/former employers and associates. Purpose : Personal information is used to support decisions for granting or reviewing for cause the reliability status, security clearance or site access of individuals working or applying to work through appointment, assignment or contract. A review for cause may result in the revoking of the individual’s reliability status, security clearance or site access. Personal information is collected pursuant to paragraph 30(1) (a) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act and as required under the Policy on Government Security. Consistent Uses: Where applicable, information, including fingerprints, may be shared with the RCMP and CSIS to conduct the requisite checks and/or investigation in accordance with the Policy on Government Security; refer to the following institution-specific personal information banks: for the RMCP (Forensic Science and Identification Services and Canadian Criminal Real Time Identification Services CMP PPU 030), for CSIS (Security Assessments/Advice SIS PPU 005). The security screening status may be shared with Human Resources officials to update the individual's personnel file, refer to Standard Personal Information Bank Employee Personnel Record - PSE 901. Information may be shared, with the following internal CRA Programs to verify individual’s compliance with tax laws: Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Collections (CRA PPU 050). Information may be shared with entities outside the federal government, including credit bureaus. Some information may be used or disclosed for program evaluation. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records are retained at a private-sector contractor records storage facility for 2 years after the last administrative action and then are destroyed. If the records contain adverse information and/or the individual’s security status or clearance was denied or revoke, they are retained for 10 years after the last administrative action and then are destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: PRN 920 and PRN 931 TBS Registration Number: 20140040 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 917

### Recovery Benefits: Canada Recovery Benefit, Canada Recovery Sickness Benefit, and Canada Recovery Caregiving Benefit
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the Canada Recovery Benefit, Canada Recovery Sickness Benefit and Canada Recovery Caregiving Benefit, income support payments for workers affected by the COVID-19 (coronavirus) pandemic. Personal information may include: name, contact information, mailing address, social insurance number, banking information, date of birth, date of death, eligibility indicators, application details, and incarceration status. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide their social insurance number. Information may be stored in CRA internal systems or databases, including: Agency Management System /Credential Management System AMS/CMS, T1 IDENT system, My Account Portal, HCCS IVR, TSAD-I , BI Renewal, and the Direct Deposit enrolment service. Class of Individuals : Individuals who apply for the Canada Recovery Benefit, Canada Recovery Sickness Benefit, or Canada Recovery Caregiving Benefit. Purpose: Personal information is used to determine an individual's eligibility for the Canada Recovery Benefit, Canada Recovery Sickness Benefit, and Canada Recovery Caregiving Benefit, for authorizing payment, and to ensure compliance. Personal information is collected under the authority of the Canada Recovery Benefit Act, and the delegation instrument issued pursuant to section 11 of the Department of Employment and Social Development Act to the Canada Revenue Agency in respect of the Canada Recovery Benefit Act, signed October 2, 2020, as well as section 241(5)(vii.6) of the Income Tax Act. The social insurance number is collected under section 25 of the Canada Recovery Benefit Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation and/or signed information-sharing agreements, including Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments PWGSC PCU 712), Employment and Social Development Canada (Employment Insurance Claim Files ESDC PPU 151), Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data – Evaluation and Formulation of fiscal Policy FIN PPU 100) and Statistics Canada for statistical analysis. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs including: Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Individual Returns Compliance (PIB under development), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Non-Filer Compliance, CRA PPU 025, Trust Accounts Compliance (CRA PPU 120), Leads Program (CRA PPU 423), and with Public Communications (PSU 914). Information may also be used or shared for other purposes such as: for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g., audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and statistical analysis), strategy development, and reports. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records are retained for 6 years after the tax year in which the benefit is filed and are then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority : 2017/012 (replaces 92/005) Related Class of Record Number : CRA ABSB 649 TBS Registration Number : 20220006 Personal Information Bank Number : CRA PPU 642

### Registered Deferred Income and Savings Plans
Description: This bank describes personal information relating to the registration and monitoring of registered deferred income and savings plans, including: registered pension plans, deferred profit sharing plans, registered education savings plans, registered disability savings plans, supplementary unemployment benefit plans, registered retirement savings plans, registered retirement income funds, registered investments, tax-free savings accounts, pooled registered pension plans, and first home savings accounts. Personal information may include name, contact information, social insurance number, other identification numbers (e.g. specimen plan number, insurance policy number, business number), financial information, signature, medical information, language, citizenship status, gender, biographical information, date of birth, and date of death. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide social insurance number, business number, and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This personal information bank was updated in 2025. Class of Individuals : Individual taxpayers including family members (spouses, dependents, common-law partners) and their representatives, primary caregivers, holders, individuals who act as or are contacts for: employers, trustees, agents, brokers, insurers and those insured, nominees, insurance companies, trust companies, financial institutions, and pension plan administrators, subscribers, members, successor members, survivors, beneficiaries, and promoters of registered deferred income and savings plans. Purpose: Personal information is collected under the authority of the Income Tax Act and is used to register and monitor deferred income and savings plans in accordance with the Income Tax Act and related regulations and rules. The social insurance number is collected in accordance with the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with other federal public bodies in accordance with legislation, including Employment and Social Development Canada (Canada Education Savings Program ESDC PPU 506, Canada Disability Savings Program ESDC PPU 038), Statistics Canada (Classes of Personal Information), and the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (Classes of Personal Information). Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed information sharing agreements, and with plan administrators. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs including Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), T3 Assessment Program (CRA PPU 015), Tax-Free Savings Initiatives (CRA PPU 054), and Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005). Information may also be used or disclosed for audit, program evaluation, strategy development, research, data matching and statistical purposes. Retention and Disposal Standards: Paper records are maintained at a private contractor records storage facility. Information is retained for as long as the plan is registered. Once the plan is terminated, the information will be retained for an additional 10 years following the Agency’s final correspondence and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA LPRAB 117 TBS Registration Number: 20090532 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 226
Description : This bank describes personal information related to requests for remission consideration. Remission is a rare and extraordinary measure that allows the Governor in Council to provide full or partial relief from tax, interest, penalty, or other debt, where relief is warranted. Generally, all means available within the applicable legislation should be exhausted before remission relief is considered such as an objection, appeal to the Tax Court of Canada (or the Federal Court) or a request under the taxpayer relief provisions of the Income Tax Act or the Excise Tax Act. Personal information is submitted voluntarily by the requestor or has been collected by other CRA programs in the usual course of their business activities. Personal information may include: name, biographical information, citizenship status, contact information, credit history, date of birth, date of death, education information, financial information, gender, language, medical information, place of birth, place of death, signature, social insurance number, and other identification numbers, including business number and trust number. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , taxpayers requesting information described by this bank must provide their name, address, social insurance number, business number or trust number, licence or registration number, and where applicable, written third party authorization, and the subject and date of the requested correspondence/document. Information may be stored in the following CRA internal systems or databases: Enterprise Correspondence, Correspondence and Issues Management System, Rulings and Interpretations Tracking System, and Legislative Information Tracking System. This personal information bank was updated in 2021 and includes the remission activities formerly held under GST/HST Rulings and Interpretations (CRA PPU 092). Class of Individuals : Individual requesters, their spouses or common-law partners, and/or their dependents, authorized representatives, sole proprietors, partners in a partnership, corporate officers, shareholders, trustees and beneficiaries; GST/HST registrants and rebate claimants; excise duty licensees and registrants; excise tax licensees; and registrants for other levies. Purpose : Personal information is used to review all remission requests for the relief of federal income tax, GST/HST, excise taxes, duties or other levies, penalties, interest or other debts paid or payable under legislation administered by the CRA such as the Income Tax Act and the Excise Tax Act in order to make a recommendation to the Director General, Assistant Commissioner or Minister. Personal information is also used to prepare a draft remission order, examined by the Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010), and then submitted to the Commissioner and the Minister, in turn, for their approval. Only then will the submission be sent to the Privy Council Office to be brought before the Treasury Board (Governor in Council) for consideration. The SIN is collected pursuant to the Income Tax Act and will be used for identification purposes only. The subsections 23(2) and 23(2.1) of the Financial Administration Act provides the Governor in Council with the legislative authority to grant remission on the recommendation of the appropriate Minister. The Minister of National Revenue is the appropriate Minister to consider remission of amounts under legislation administered by the CRA such as the Income Tax Act and the Excise Tax Act. Consistent Uses : The information may be accessed during the research and analysis of remission requests. Information may also be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with section 241 of the Income Tax Act, the Income Tax Regulations and section 295 of the Excise Tax Act including: Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data – Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy, FIN PPU 100) and Employment and Social Development Canada (Universal Child Care Benefit, ESDC PPU 102). Information (or part thereof) may also be disclosed to other applicable provincial/territorial governments in accordance with signed taxpayer or registrant information sharing agreements such as the Ontario Ministry of Finance. Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, and reports. ### Scientific Research and Experimental Development
Description: This bank describes information related to the verification of qualifying requirements of claims filed by individuals or businesses under the Scientific Research and Experimental Development Program, and identification of non-compliance with the legislative requirements, tax laws, policies and procedures. Personal information may include name (first and last names), contact information (address, phone number, fax number, and email address), social insurance number, other identification numbers (business number, GST/HST number, and trust account number), biographical information (qualifications, education and experience, position, title and trade of individuals directly involved in the scientific research and experimental development project(s)), financial information (salary and wages, and payments made to a third party) and signature. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the social insurance number, business number, or trust account number and legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. . This personal information bank was updated in 2024. Class of Individuals: Scientific Research and Experimental Development claimants including owners of sole proprietorships, partners in a partnership, and individuals involved in the projects being claimed, authorized corporate officers, contact persons, representatives and designated authorities, employees, external consultants, and claim preparers. Purpose: Personal information is collected pursuant to sections 37, 150, 162 and 248(1) of the Income Tax Act and is used for verification of compliance, administration and enforcement of the Scientific Research and Experimental Development Program requirements. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with income tax legislation, including Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data – Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy, FIN PPU 100) and Statistics Canada (for statistical data). Information may also be shared with provincial government institutions in accordance with the Memorandum of Understanding between the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) and the province regarding the administration of the provincial research and development tax credits. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs/Activities, including Individual Returns and Payment Processing, (CRA PPU 005), Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), T3 Assessment program (CRA PPU 015), Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421) and, Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120). Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics and reports. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting. The social insurance number may be disclosed to other federal institutions for the purpose of administration and enforcement of the Income Tax Act to the extent authorized by law. Retention and Disposal Standards: T1 records are retained for 5 years after the last administrative transaction at a private-sector contractor records storage facility, and then destroyed. T2 records, T3 records and partnership records are retained for 10 years after the last administrative transaction at a private-sector contractor records storage facility, and then destroyed. All other information obtained from outside sources for risk assessment purposes that do not lead to an administrative action will be destroyed after 2 years. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA CPB 155 TBS Registration Number: 20140076 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 441

### Service Feedback Program
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the processing of service-related complaints, including problem resolution, and referrals to the Ombudsperson Liaison Office regarding a variety of issues that have not been resolved through regular procedures, including the quality and timeliness of service provided by CRA. Personal information may include: name, contact information, biographical information, language, citizenship status, , date of birth, date of death, financial information, medical information, employment information, signature, social insurance number, business number, other identification numbers and opinion or views of, or about, individuals. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide: proof of identification showing an individual's or business' name, address, identification number and/or, in the case of a representative, proof of authorization. This PIB was updated in 2024. Class of Individuals : Individuals, businesses and benefit recipients including non-residents, or their authorized representatives, who submit a formal service-related complaint to the CRA or the Office of the Taxpayers’ Ombudsperson, or request assistance with an issue that has not been resolved through regular procedures, or submit a compliment or suggestion to the CRA related to services. Purpose: Personal information is collected for the purpose of resolving a complaint or problem, or ensuring a compliment or suggestions are considered by the CRA. Personal information is collected under the authority of section 5 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act which gives to the CRA the mandate to support the administration and enforcement of the legislation under the responsibility of the Minister of National Revenue, including Part I of the Canada Pension Plan, Part IV of the Employment Insurance Act, collection of debts under the Canada Student Financial Assistance Act and the Canada Student Loans Act. More specifically, section 220 of the Income Tax Act and section 275 of the Excise Tax Act authorize the CRA to collect personal information for purposes related to the administration and enforcement of these acts. Personal information collected is used to administer the Service Feedback Program. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and section 275 of the Excise Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with written consent by the taxpayer (individual and business), benefit recipient and/or their representative, including Members of Parliament.  Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs/activities, including CRA program areas and regional offices involved in the complaint, as well as Ministerial correspondence officials for routing of response and the Office of the Taxpayers’ Ombudsperson (CRA PPU 222). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as: follow-up, creating an inventory of complaints, corporate reporting, evaluation, and to identify and analyze systemic service-related issues and trends. Information may also be disclosed to other applicable provincial/territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed taxpayer information sharing agreements. Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, data matching, strategy development, statistics and reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards : Personal information from a service feedback record is retained for two (2) years after the last administrative use and then destroyed.
Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA APP 575 TBS Registration Number: 002024 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 571

### Small and Medium Enterprises
Description : This bank describes personal information related to administration and enforcement of the Income Tax Act, Excise Tax Act, and Underused Housing Tax Act including identification of non-compliance, audit enforcement activities, taxpayer assistance, and education pertaining to small and medium businesses, and to non-resident taxpayers. The information is received from individuals, trusts, partnerships, and corporations, and includes, but is not limited to, information received through income tax returns, information returns, and forms. Personal information may also be collected during an audit and indirectly from sources other than the taxpayer, including open-source Internet checks. This may include collecting and verifying information from federal and provincial departments or agencies, the private sector, and public sources including newspapers and the Internet. Personal information may include name, contact information, citizenship, language, credit history, date of birth, date of death, financial information, biographical information, medical information, risk profiles, criminal history, gender, Business Number (BN), trust number, partnership number, signature, and Social Insurance Number (SIN). Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request Form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide: the SIN, BN, and legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This PIB was updated in 2025. Class of Individuals : Individual taxpayers including non-residents, family members (dependents, spouses, common-law partners), sole proprietors, partners in a partnership, other business owners, corporate officers, representatives and designated authorities, agents, trustees, settlors, and beneficiaries. Purpose : Personal information is used for the following purpose:  to administer the small and medium enterprises compliance program with respect to the Income Tax Act, the Excise Tax Act, and the Underused Housing Tax Act. Compliance activities include: identification of non-compliance; risk assessment; audit; taxpayer assistance; and education. Personal information is collected under the authority of: the Income Tax Act and the Income Tax Regulations; the Excise Tax Act; and the Underused Housing Tax Act. The Social Insurance Number is collected pursuant to the Income Tax Act and will be used for identification purposes only. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with income tax legislation and/or signed information-sharing agreements, including: Canada Border Services Agency (Administrative Monetary Penalty System Program, CBSA PPU 001), Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data – Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy, FIN PPU 100); Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010); Public Prosecution Service of Canada (National Fine Recovery Program, PPSC PPU 001); Office of the Auditor General of Canada (Performance Audit Files, OAG PPU 002); Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada(Compliance of Reporting Entities, FINTRAC PPU 028); and Royal Canadian Mounted Police (Operational Case Records, RCMP PPU 005). Information may also be disclosed to  provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed memoranda of understanding. Personal information obtained about residents of countries with which Canada has a comprehensive income tax conventions or Tax information exchange agreements – Canada.ca may be shared with those taxing authorities by way of an exchange of information under the appropriate treaty provision. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs, including: Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005); Tax-Free Savings Account (CRA PPU 054); T3 Assessment program (CRA PPU 015); Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047); Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223); Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) (CRA PPU 140); Commodity Tax Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 177); Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172); Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580); Collections  (CRA PPU 050); Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025); Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120); Detection and Investigations (CRA PPU 095); Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085); International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035); Scientific Research and Experimental Development (CRA PPU 441);  Service Complaints (CRA PPU 571); GST/HST Audit and Examination (CRA PPU 430); Charities Program (CRA PPU 200); and Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220). Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as: the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics and reporting. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow-through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting and workload development. Retention and Disposal Standards : Audit files records will be retained for ten years after the appeal process is resolved and then will be destroyed.  All other records obtained from outside sources for risk assessment purposes that do not lead to an administrative action will be retained at a private-sector records storage facility for 2 years and then are destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number : CRA CPB 452 TBS Registration Number: 20140083 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 421

### Specialty Business Returns

This bank also describes information concerning taxpayer payment processing options, including pre-authorized debit. Personal information may include name, contact information, financial information, signature, language, citizenship status, social insurance number (SIN), business number (BN), and trust number. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide the SIN, BN, trust number and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. Information may also be shared with provincial and territorial governments (or parts thereof) to the extent authorized by law. Information may also be used or shared for other purposes such as: for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, statistics and reports. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching purposes land knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained at a private-sector contractor records facility as permanent documents for businesses involved with the import, manufacture, and distribution of tobacco products. Records for the T5013 partnerships will be retained for 5 years. Records for Excise Duty Returns, Excise Tax Returns, Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Returns, Air Travellers Security Charge Returns, and Fuel Charge forms will be retained for 5 years. Payment records will be retains for 7 years with the exception of pre-authorized debit requests which are retain at a private-sector contractor records storage facility for 10 years and is then destroyed.
