### Subsidy Initiatives For Business Entities
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the processing, assessment, and validation of the COVID-19 Subsidy Initiatives for Business Entities. The Subsidy Initiatives for Business Entities are composed of the Canada Emergency Wage Subsidy, the Canada Recovery Hiring Program, the Canada Emergency Rent Subsidy, the Hardest-Hit Business Recovery Program, and the Tourism and Hospitality Recovery Program. These programs are COVID-19-related initiatives aimed at enabling eligible employers who have experienced reductions in revenue with a subsidy to cover part of their wage expenses allowing employers to hire new employees or to re-hire workers previously laid off as a result of COVID-19 and to help with rent or property expenses. Personal information may include: name, business number, contact information, financial information, employment information,  signature, attestation, and internet protocol address. Note :  In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide the necessary authorization and a business number. This PIB was updated in 2023. Pending approval. Class of Individuals : Individuals, sole proprietors, partners in partnerships, authorized corporate officers, and authorized representatives. Purpose : Personal information is used for the following purpose:  to determine a business’s  eligibility and entitlement, process, assess and validate the Covid 19 subsidy initiatives for business entities through processing and validation activities. Validation activities may include authorized automated or manual personal information analysis to verify the accuracy of the information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to maintain the integrity of the programs. Personal information is also used for payment processing purposes and also to undertake measures to recoup funds that should not have been paid.  Personal information is collected under the authority of section 125.7 of the Income Tax Act. Section 61 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act allows the CRA to implement agreements with other federal, provincial, and territorial governments for the purpose of carrying out an activity or program administered by the CRA. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation and/or signed information-sharing agreements, including Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data - Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy, FIN PPU 100) and Statistics Canada for statistical analysis, and Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments, PWGSC PCU 712) for the issuance of payments. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs, including: Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223), Specialty Business Returns (CRA PPU 224), Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), GST/HST Returns and Rebates Program (CRA PPU 241), and Collections (CRA PPU 050), Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), T3 Assessment Program (CRA PPU 015), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Advance Income Tax Rulings and Technical Interpretation (CRA PPU 090). Information may also be used or shared for other purposes such as: for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g., audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and statistical analysis), strategy development, and reports. Retention and Disposal Standards : Records are retained for 6 years after the last administrative action and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority : 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number : CRA ABSB 230 TBS Registration Number : 20240001 Personal Information Bank Number : CRA PPU 231

### T3 Assessment program
Description: This bank describes personal information that is collected in relation to the creation of and assessment of trust accounts. Personal information may include: name, contact information, date of birth, date of death, social insurance number, other identification numbers, trust account number, business number, language preference, residency status, biographical information, signature and financial information. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide: trust account number, signature, and legal authority for those acting on behalf of the trustee or custodian. Information may be stored in CRA internal systems or databases. This PIB was updated in 2025. Class of Individuals: Settlor (the person setting up the trust), beneficiaries of a trust and trustees which can include an executor, administrator, assignee, receiver, custodian or liquidator who owns or controls property for some other person, and their representatives. Purpose: Personal information is used to facilitate the verification, validation, processing, assessment and reassessment of T3 Trust Income Tax Returns and related schedules and Special Returns and the collection of taxes pertaining to trusts. Personal information is collected under the authority of section 150 and section 220 of the Income Tax Act. The Social Insurance Number is collected pursuant to 248(1) definition of graduated rate estate (c) of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with the income tax act, such as Statistics Canada (Administrative Data, StatCan PPU 136), Department of Finance (for the formulation and evaluation of fiscal policy), Public Service and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments, PWGSC PCU 712), Department of Justice (Family Orders and Agreements Enforcement Assistance, JUS PPU 125), Agriculture and Agri-food Canada (Net Income Stabilization Account, AAFC PPU 183), Office of the Auditor General of Canada (Performance audits, OAG PPU 002), Employment and Social Development Canada (Canada Pension Plan Program ESDC PPU 146, Old Age Security Program ESDC PPU 116, Employment Insurance Databank ESDC PPU 501) and third party storage providers Iron Mountain and Nimble Inc for record storage. Information may also be disclosed to other applicable provincial/territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed taxpayer information sharing agreements. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs/Activities, including: Advance Income Tax Rulings and Technical Interpretations (CRA PPU 090), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223), Detection and investigations (CRA PPU 095), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Non-filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Part XIII Non-resident Withholding Program (CRA PPU 094), International and Large Business Income Tax Audit and Examination (CRA PPU 035), and Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571). Personal information may also be used or disclosed for CRA corporate purposes, including the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, and strategy development, and may be used for data-matching purposes, reporting and for survey or other statistical analysis purposes.
Retention and Disposal Standards: All records, regardless of format or medium are retained for 5 years. Paper records are then destroyed. Electronic destruction is under development. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA ABSB 139 TBS Registration Number: 003536 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 015

### Tax-Free Savings Initiatives
Description : This bank describes information related to the Tax-Free Savings initiatives such as the Tax-Free Savings Account program that allows individuals who are 18 and older to set money aside tax-free throughout their lifetime; and the Tax-Free First Home Savings Account program, a new registered account savings initiative created to help individuals save for their first home; including: yearly transaction details for each Tax-Free Savings Initiative and information on the validation, processing, and assessment of the Tax-Free Savings Initiative records and returns. Personal information may include: name, contact information, date of birth, date of death, marital status, citizenship, financial information, language preference, signature, social insurance number, and other identification numbers. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the social insurance number, temporary tax number, Tax-Free Savings Account number and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. Information may be stored in CRA internal systems or databases. This personal information bank was updated in 2024. Class of Individuals : Individuals (other than a trust) who are 18 years of age or older, and who have a valid Canadian social insurance number or temporary tax number, their spouses or common-law partners, their dependents, their representatives, beneficiaries, successors, and other individuals who act as contacts for issuers. Purpose : Personal information is collected under the authority of section 146.2 and 146.6 of the Income Tax Act and section 219 and 223 of the Income Tax Regulations and is used to administer the Tax-Free Savings initiatives, including: determining eligibility; ensuring that the Tax-Free initiative information transmitted from issuers is correctly associated with the appropriate individuals; ensuring that relevant information in CRA systems is accurate and updated appropriately; calculating the participation room, unused contribution room; identifying and assessing potentially applicable taxes and deductions, penalties and interest. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal or provincial government institutions in accordance with legislation and formal information sharing agreements, including the Department of Finance Canada (Tax Data - Evaluation and Formulation of Fiscal Policy FIN PPU 100), Public Service and Procurement Canada (PWGSC PPU 712) for payment issuance. Information may also be disclosed to Revenu Quebec to administer an adjustment to the provincial income tax and benefit return. Information may also be shared with internal CRA programs/activities, including: Income Tax and Charity Objections, Determinations and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Collections CRA PPU 050, Voluntary disclosures program (CRA PPU 220), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Detection and Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Contact Centre Operations(Public Communications CRA PSU 914) and the Security Branch to investigate possible misuse or abuse of the program and systems involved. Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), strategy development, data matching, statistics and reporting and business intelligence activities for analytics, research, and policy evaluation. Retention and Disposal Standards : All electronically stored forms are held for a period of 10 years and then destroyed. Paper forms that are stored in hard copy will be retained for a period of 10 years and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority : 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number : CRA ABSB 210 TBS Registration Number : 20150053 Personal Information Bank Number : CRA PPU 054

### Taxpayer Relief Program
Description : This bank describes personal information related to taxpayers who request relief from penalties and interest. This also describes personal information related to taxpayers who request relief from charges related to the Québec Sales Tax for selected listed financial institutions. Personal information may include: name, biographical information, contact information, financial information, credit history, credit card information, signature, employment status, date of birth, date of death, language, medical information, social insurance number, business numbers and other identification numbers. Note : This personal information bank was updated in 2024. Class of Individuals : Individuals, corporations, employers, importers and exporters, estates and trusts, GST/HST filers, non-residents, and selected listed financial institution Québec Sales Tax filers. Purpose : Personal information, including the social insurance number, is collected under the Income Tax Act, Excise Tax Act, Air Travellers Security Charge Act, Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006, Excise Act, 2001, Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, Customs Act, Underused Housing Tax Act, Select Luxury Items Tax Act, Digital Services Tax Act, Global Minimum Tax Act, and Quebec Tax Administration Act to process requests for relief of penalties, interest, as well as requests for relief of Québec Sales Tax charges for selected listed financial institutions. It may also be used for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of these Acts. With the exception of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act and the non-GST/HST provisions of the Excise Tax Act, information may be shared or verified with other federal, provincial/territorial government institutions as authorized by law. Consistent Uses : Information may be used to record taxpayer relief requests, their analysis and decision. Information may also be used or disclosed for other purposes, such as: to produce various reports, including financial reports, for the Federal Government and the provinces and territories, for relief granted under the provisions, as required by section 24.2 of the Financial Administration Act. Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with income tax and excise legislation, including the Department of Justice Canada (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services JUS PPU 010) and the Canada Border Services Agency.  Information may also be disclosed to other applicable provincial/territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed taxpayer information sharing agreements such as Revenu Québec. Personal information may be verified with open-source Internet checks, other public sources and credit bureaus. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA programs/activities, such as: Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Individual Returns and payment processing (CRA PPU 005), Corporation Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), Specialty Business Returns (CRA PPU 224) , GST/HST Returns and Rebates Program (CRA PPU 241), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223) , Employer Compliance (CRA PPU 120), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220) , GST / HST Audit and Examination (CRA PPU 430), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Charities Program (CRA PPU 200), Registered Deferred Income and Savings Plans (CRA PPU 226), Excise and Specialty Tax (CRA PPU 062), Remissions (CRA PPU 142), Part XIII Non-Resident Withholding Program (CRA PPU 094), T3 Assessment program (CRA PPU 015) and Monitoring of electronic access to taxpayer information (CRA PPU 718). Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (for example audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management, data matching, strategy development, statistics and reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards : Paper records will be retained for 10 years after the last administrative action. Case files will be retained for 10 years after the last issuance of a decision. Records may then be destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA APP 585 TBS Registration Number: 003943 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 580

### Underused Housing Tax
Description : This bank describes personal information related to the processing, assessment, reassessment, endorsing, verification and validation and posting of personal information filed as a requirement of the Underused Housing Tax Act. This may include collecting and/or verifying information from federal departments or agencies. This bank also describes information concerning taxpayer payment processing options. Personal information may include: name, contact information, financial information, signature, language, social insurance number, business number, individual tax number, temporary tax number, citizenship, residential property information and internet protocol address. Note :  In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals requesting information described by this bank may need to provide the SIN, BN, ITN and/or authorization for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This PIB was created in 2025. Class of Individuals : Individual taxpayers, spouses or common-law partners, trustees, receivers, corporations, shareholders, representatives and designated authorities, and non-residents. Purpose : Personal information is used to administer the Underused Housing Tax program, including to determine or verify the person's liability for underused housing tax and to process payments. Personal information is collected under the authority of sections 7(1), 8(a) and 10 of the Underused Housing Tax Act. In addition, paragraph 84(1)(b) of the Underused Housing Tax Act grants authority for the Governor in Council to enact regulations requiring any person to provide any information to any class of persons required to make a return under the Underused Housing Tax Act containing that information, although no regulations have been enacted to date pursuant to this authority. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with signed information-sharing agreements, including Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments PWGSC PCU 712) and Department of Justice (Civil Proceedings and Legal Service JUS PPU 010). Information may be shared with Iron Mountain and Nimble Inc. for secure storage purposes. Information may also be shared, as applicable, with internal CRA Programs including: Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Service Feedback (CRA PPU 571), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), T3 Assessment program (CRA PPU 015), Authentication and Credential Management Service (CRA PPU 607), Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Collections (CRA PPU 050), Voluntary Disclosures Program (CRA PPU 220), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), National Security Disclosures (CRA PPU 202), Underused Housing Tax Compliance (CRA PPU 214), Security Incidents and Privacy Breaches (PSU 939), Electronic Network Monitoring Logs (PSU 905), Competent Authority (CRA PPU 085), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), International and Large Business, and High Net Worth Compliance Tax Audit Programs (CRA PPU 035), Advance Income Tax Rulings and Technical Interpretations (CRA PPU 090), EFILE Online Services (CRA PPU 211), Monitoring of Electronic Access to Taxpayer Information (CRA PPU 718), Remissions (CRA PPU 142), Leads Program (CRA PPU 423), and to the Office of the Taxpayers' Ombudsperson (CRA PPU 222). Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (data matching, quality assurance and data integrity), statistics, compliance and enforcement risk management, business intelligence, strategy development, and reports. Retention and Disposal Standards : Records will be retained for 10 years after activity is complete and then destroyed. Record Disposition Authority : 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number : CRA ABSB 215 TBS Registration Number : 20250401 Personal Information Bank Number : CRA PPU 214

### Voluntary Disclosures Program
Description : This bank describes personal information related to disclosures made by taxpayers who have voluntarily come forward to correct inaccurate or incomplete information or to disclose information they have not reported during previous dealings with the CRA. Taxpayers who make a valid disclosure, in accordance with the CRA criteria, will have to pay the taxes owing, plus interest in part or in full. However, taxpayers would be eligible for relief from prosecution and, in some cases, from penalties that they would otherwise be subject to under the legislation. Personal information may include: name, contact information, social insurance number, other identification number (e.g. business number, trust account number, non-resident account number, individual tax number), date of death, signatures and financial/tax information such as: income, benefits, investments, financial statements, personal and investment property. Additional personal information may be collected indirectly from sources other than the taxpayer, including public sources. Note: In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , , individuals requesting information described by this bank must provide the social insurance number, business number, trust account number, non-resident number and/or legal authority for those acting on behalf of an account holder or estate. This PIB was updated in 2023. Class of Individuals: Taxpayers or their authorized representative, sole proprietors, partners in a partnership, trustees and other business owners who have made a disclosure of non-compliance with respect to Income Tax and Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax Credit (GST/HST credit) and related benefits and credits purposes, as well as for charges under the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006, the Air Travelers Security Charge Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act and the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act. Purpose: Personal information is used to determine if the voluntary disclosure of errors or omissions not previously reported by taxpayers meet the eligibility requirements of a valid disclosure and therefore entitle the applicant to receive relief from some penalties, and in some cases, partial interest relief, that the taxpayers would otherwise be subject to under the Acts noted above, and avoid prosecution. Personal information is collected under the authority of sections 150 and 220 of the Income Tax Act, sections 149, 175, 186, 215, 238 and 275 of the Excise Tax Act, sections 8, 160 and 161 of the Excise Act 2001, section 4 of the Credit for Provincial Relief (HST) Regulations SOR/2011-57, sections 8 and 17 of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, sections 19 and 26 of the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006, subsection 92(1) of the Select Luxury Items Tax Act, section 70 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, 2018, and subsection 33(1) of the Underused Housing Tax Act. The social insurance number is collected pursuant to section 237 of the Income Tax Act and Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, and is used for identification purposes and to verify identity in respect of the business number, where applicable. Consistent Uses: Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation and formal information sharing agreements, including the Department of Justice Canada (Civil Proceedings and Legal Services, JUS PPU 010). Information may also be disclosed to other applicable provincial/territorial governments (or parts thereof) in accordance with signed taxpayer information sharing agreements. Information may also be shared with internal CRA programs/activities, including: Monitoring of Electronic Access to Taxpayer Information (CRA PPU 718), GST/HST Returns and Rebates Processing (CRA PPU 241), Corporate Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 047), Individual Returns and Payment Processing (CRA PPU 005), T3 Assessment program (CRA PPU 015), Specialty Business Returns (CRA PPU 224), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Non-Filer Compliance (CRA PPU 025), Business Number and Program Account Registration (CRA PPU 223), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580), Underused Housing Tax Program (CRA PPU 214), and Part XIII Non-Resident Withholding Program (CRA PPU 094). Information may also be used or disclosed for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance and data integrity), compliance and enforcement risk management data matching, strategy development, statistics and reporting. Retention and Disposal Standards: Records will be retained for seven years and then are destroyed. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number: CRA CPB 264 TBS Registration Number: 20090529 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 220

### Working Income Tax Benefit
Description : This bank describes personal information about individuals who apply for Working Income Tax Benefit Advanced Payments. Personal information may include: name, contact information, date of birth, financial information, employer information, biographical information, social insurance number, and signature. Note : In addition to the requirements specified on the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat Personal Information Request form , individuals and representatives as authorize in sections 241(4)d)(ii) and 241 (4)d )(iii) of the Income Tax Act, requesting information described by this bank can get the information, when providing the social insurance number, or the representative number and/ or proof of authorized designated official. Information may be stored in the following Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) internal systems or databases: Individual Credit Determination databank which records all applicable financial transactions and the reasons why a payment may have been withheld (e.g. debt owing to the Crown). In addition, the databank records any subsequent activity on an individual's Working Income Tax Benefit credit account (e.g. changed eligibility or entitlement and additional cheque issued). This personal information bank was updated in 2019. Class of Individuals : Individuals, or their authorized representatives, who apply for the Working Income Tax Benefit Advance payments, and their family members (dependents, spouses, common-law partners). Purpose : Personal information is used to determine an individual's eligibility and entitlement for the Working Income Tax Benefit Advanced payments through processing, and validation activities. Validation activities may include authorized automated personal information analysis to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA. Personal information is also used for payment processing purposes and payment set-offs. The authority to perform a set-off is granted under subsection 164(2) of the Income Tax Act and subsection 155(1) of the Financial Administration Act. Personal information is collected under the authority of sections 122.7, 220(1) and 241 of Income Tax Act. The social insurance number is collected under section 237 of the Income Tax Act and is used for identification purposes. Consistent Uses : Information may be shared with other federal government institutions in accordance with legislation including Public Services and Procurement Canada (Receiver General Payments, PWGSC PCU 712) and Employment and Social Development Canada (Canada Pension Plan Program (ESDC PPU 146), with Veterans Affairs Canada (Earnings Loss VAC PPU 607 and Canadian Forces Income Supplement VAC PPU 608), and Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (Application and Assessment for Canadian Citizenship CIC PPU 050) for the processing of payments. Information may be shared with other federal or provincial/territorial governments under existing Memoranda of Understanding or information-sharing agreements with CRA. Information may also be shared with other CRA programs for the purpose of validating information in order to support eligibility and to facilitate correct calculation of payments including: Canada Child Benefit and Related Benefits (CRA PPU 063), Disability Tax Credit Program (CRA PPU 218), and Individual Returns and payment processing (CRA PPU 005), and with other applicable programs within CRA such as Collections (CRA PPU 050), Small and Medium Enterprises (CRA PPU 421), Criminal Investigations (CRA PPU 095), Office of the Taxpayers' Ombudsman (CRA PPU 222), Service Feedback Program (CRA PPU 571), Taxpayer Relief Program (CRA PPU 580) and Income Tax and Charity Objections, and Appeals to the Courts (CRA PPU 172). Information may also be shared with third parties upon consent of the taxpayer for income verification purposes. Information may also be used or shared for other purposes such as: for the evaluation and supervision of CRA officials in the administration of their duties under the relevant Acts (e.g. audit trails), program evaluation (quality assurance, data integrity and statistical analysis), compliance and enforcement risk management, strategy development, and reports. Risk management activities may include any authorized automated personal information analysis, personal information matching and knowledge discovery techniques to verify the accuracy of personal information against a reliable source within or outside the CRA, to verify flow through reporting, and business intelligence and risk analysis in order to identify non-compliant reporting. Information may also be used to determine whether an individual knowingly participated in or made a false statement or omission. The consequences can include reviews which may result in termination and/or recoup of benefits, and possibly levying civil penalties under section 163(2) of the Income Tax Act. Also, in limited cases, information obtained during the course of a validation or compliance review could be used to refer the matter to the Criminal Investigations Division of the CRA for further investigation which could result in the laying of criminal charges under section 238 or section 239 of the Income Tax Act against a particular individual. Retention and Disposal Standards : Records will be retained for a period of five years following the last administrative action and then destroyed. Income verification request file are retain for two years and response files are kept for 180 days on tape by the Information and Technology Branch, at which point they are purged, and reused as necessary. Record Disposition Authority: 2017/012 Related Class of Record Number : CRA ABSB 346 TBS Registration Number : 20091359 Personal Information Bank Number: CRA PPU 178
